name: Accordion Fold Reveal
behavior: Drag a header down and the panel below unfolds in accordion bellows, each pleat catching light differently as it expands, with the fold count tied to drag distance.
wow: Light glints across each pleat as it opens, so the expansion reads as a 3D paper bellows rather than a height animation.
mechanism: DragGesture y-distance sets unfold progress; a VStack of pleat strips each apply rotation3DEffect about their top edge with alternating angles, each strip's LinearGradient brightness keyed to its fold angle for the glint; .spring on release. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView eases unfold progress 0→1→0; pleats rotate open one row at a time with traveling light glints, then fold shut—auto bellows.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged maps translation.height to unfold 0–1 driving each pleat's rotation3DEffect angle and gradient brightness; onEnded snaps open/closed with .spring(bounce:0.25).

name: Crumple-to-Dismiss
behavior: Pinch a note and it crumples inward into a wrinkled paper ball with creasing shadows, shrinking as you squeeze, then on release it tosses off-screen with a tumbling spin.
wow: The flat note gains procedural crease shading as it balls up, making deletion feel like genuinely scrunching paper.
mechanism: MagnifyGesture inverse-scales the note while a Canvas overlays procedural crease facets (random polygon shards with light/dark fills) whose opacity rises with crumple progress; release runs a .spring toss with rotation3DEffect tumble and offset off-screen. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.flat, .crumpled, .tossed] auto-shrinks the note while crease facets fade in, then tumbles it off-tile and resets—self-crumpling loop.
interactiveSpec: MagnifyGesture().onChanged maps (1-magnification) to crumple progress shrinking the note and revealing Canvas crease shading; onEnded if past threshold springs a tumbling toss off-screen + sensoryFeedback(.impact), else unfolds back flat.
risk: Canvas crease facets only — no Metal; keep facet count low and randomize once (seeded) so creases don't shimmer between frames.

name: Elastic Grid Warp
behavior: Drag a point on a uniform dot grid and nearby dots pull toward it with falloff, the lattice warping like a stretched fishnet and rippling back to flat on release.
wow: The grid deforms with smooth distance falloff into a pinched well, recreating a warped-space gravity-lens feel.
mechanism: DragGesture point displaces each lattice node by a Gaussian/inverse-distance falloff toward (or away from) the touch, drawn as dots + connecting lines in Canvas; release relaxes all nodes to rest with .interpolatingSpring(bounce:0.4) for the ripple-back. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView moves a virtual warp point around the grid; nodes pull in with falloff into a traveling pinch-well and relax behind it—auto gravity-lens loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged sets the warp center; each node offset = falloff(distance)*pull rendered in Canvas; onEnded springs all nodes back to the uniform lattice with a rippling bouncy spring.
risk: Canvas lattice math only (no Metal); recomputing many nodes per frame can jank — keep grid ~16×16 max and use a cheap falloff; verify on device.

name: Tilt-Pour Liquid Gauge
behavior: Drag to tilt a container and the liquid surface stays level to gravity, sloshing with a wobbling meniscus and trailing wave that settles when you stop tilting.
wow: The fluid surface convincingly keeps horizontal and sloshes with inertia, so tilting really pours the level around.
mechanism: DragGesture sets a tilt angle; the liquid is an Animatable Shape clipped to the container whose top edge is a tilted sine wave (counter-rotated to stay level to gravity), amplitude driven by tilt velocity; .interpolatingSpring settles the slosh. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView rocks the tilt angle back and forth; the liquid surface stays level and sloshes with a trailing meniscus wave, settling between rocks—auto pour loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged maps translation to tilt; the liquid Shape's surface line counter-rotates to horizontal and its wave amplitude tracks d(tilt)/dt; onEnded settles slosh→0 with a damped spring.
risk: Animatable Shape sine surface only (no Metal); keeping the surface visually horizontal under rotation needs care — counter-rotate the wave, don't rotate the fill; verify at extreme tilts.

name: Spring Reorder List
behavior: Long-press lifts a row that tilts and casts a shadow; as you drag, other rows shove aside with damped springs and trail slightly behind the gap, settling with a soft jiggle.
wow: Displaced rows overshoot and ripple like they have mass, so reordering feels like pushing physical tiles through molasses.
mechanism: LongPressGesture.sequenced(before: DragGesture) lifts the row (scaleEffect + shadow + slight rotation3DEffect); displaced rows animate their gap offset with a lagging .interpolatingSpring(bounce:0.35) and matchedGeometryEffect for index swaps. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator scripts a fake pick-up-and-move: one row lifts, two neighbors shove aside and jiggle, row drops into the new slot, looping 0→1→0 on a TimelineView clock.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture(minDuration:0.3).sequenced(before: DragGesture()) sets a dragging id; onChanged computes target index and springs others aside; onEnded commits order with matchedGeometryEffect settle + sensoryFeedback(.impact) on pickup/drop.

name: Sag Rope Bridge
behavior: Drag a point along a taut rope and it sags into a catenary curve under the load, neighboring planks tilting to follow, snapping taut with a damped oscillation on release.
wow: The rope forms a physically correct hanging curve and twangs back, turning a slider into a load-bearing line.
mechanism: DragGesture load point drives an Animatable Shape rope Path computed as a catenary/quadratic sag through the dragged point; planks are positioned/rotated along the curve; release relaxes the sag to zero via .interpolatingSpring(bounce:0.6) for the twang. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView eases the load point side-to-side and down 0→1→0; the rope sags into a catenary, planks tilt to follow, then twangs taut—auto sag loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged sets the load position recomputing the catenary control point in the Shape's animatableData; planks sample the curve for offset/rotation; onEnded springs sag→0 with a bouncy oscillation.

name: Trampoline Net Drag
behavior: Press into a mesh net and it deforms into a stretched pocket with the surrounding grid lines pulling taut; release launches a ball upward proportional to the depression depth.
wow: The whole woven grid distorts around your finger like a real trampoline and flings the ball with stored tension.
mechanism: DragGesture point drives a depression; net grid nodes displace toward it with distance falloff (drawn as Canvas/Path lines), forming a pocket; release relaxes nodes with .interpolatingSpring(bounce:0.5) and launches a ball upward scaled by depth via a TimelineView arc. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView pushes a virtual press point down and releases; the net auto-deforms into a pocket and flings the ball up on rebound, looping the bounce.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged sets the press point and depth; grid nodes offset by falloff(distance, depth) rendered in Canvas; onEnded springs the net flat and launches the ball with velocity ∝ depth + sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: perf: a dense grid recomputed per frame in Canvas can jank — keep the net coarse (~10×10 nodes) and use a simple falloff; verify on device.

name: Edge-Swipe Page Curl
behavior: Swipe from the screen edge and the page lifts and curls back like turning a leaf, the curl radius tracking finger position and revealing the next page with a soft shadow gradient on the underside.
wow: A true cylindrical page curl with backside shading follows your finger, evoking a real book turn without any 3D libraries.
mechanism: An edge DragGesture sets the curl line x-position; the page is split into a flat remainder plus a curled strip drawn with rotation3DEffect about the curl axis and a RadialGradient/LinearGradient on its backside for cylinder shading; .spring completes or cancels the turn. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView eases the curl line from right edge across and back 0→1→0; the page lifts, curls, reveals the next, then lays flat—auto page-turn loop.
interactiveSpec: .gesture(DragGesture(minimumDistance:0)) gated to start near the trailing edge; onChanged maps location.x to curl position driving the strip's rotation3DEffect + backside gradient; onEnded commits the turn or springs back by velocity.
risk: Pure SwiftUI cylinder approximation via rotation3DEffect strip (no Metal); a single-axis fold reads convincingly but is not a true mesh curl — verify the seam shading on device.

name: Paper Plane Glide
behavior: Fling a folded paper plane and it launches along your vector, then glides in a gentle banking descent with subtle pitch bobbing before nosing down to land.
wow: Post-launch aerodynamic glide and banking give the plane a lifelike drift instead of a straight ballistic path.
mechanism: DragGesture launch velocity seeds a TimelineView glide integrator (forward velocity + lift/drag + gentle gravity) controlling offset, with rotationEffect for bank and a sine pitch-bob; eases to a nose-down landing. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-launches the plane each cycle; it glides in a banking descent with pitch bobbing and noses down to land, then resets to the launch point.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onEnded sets launch vector from velocity; glide integrator applies lift/drag for the banking descent with sine pitch-bob; lands with a small .spring settle.

name: Pinball Fling Bumpers
behavior: Fling a ball into a field of bumpers; each hit kicks it away with added energy and a flash-and-shrink reaction, the ball ricocheting until it loses speed.
wow: Energetic bumpers that pop and add speed make the ball ping around unpredictably like a real pinball table.
mechanism: DragGesture launch velocity feeds a TimelineView(.animation) integrator with circle-vs-bumper collisions that reflect and add restitution>1 energy; each hit bumper flashes brightness and scale-pops via a brief .spring; ball decays to rest. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-launches the ball each cycle; it ricochets off bumpers that flash and pop, gaining energy, then settles and re-launches—self-playing pinball.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onEnded sets launch velocity; per-frame collision detection reflects velocity off bumpers with energy gain; hit bumpers flash + scale-pop + sensoryFeedback(.impact); ball decays by friction.
risk: perf: keep bumper/ball collision math simple (circle reflections); restitution>1 can cause runaway speed — clamp velocity and verify on device.

name: Fortune Wheel Flick
behavior: Flick a segmented wheel and it spins with angular momentum, a ticking flap clacking past each segment and slowing with friction until the pointer eases into a final wedge.
wow: A clicker flap physically deflects off each passing segment, adding tactile resistance and sound cues to the spin-down.
mechanism: DragGesture angular velocity (atan2 delta) seeds a TimelineView friction-decay integrator on rotationEffect; the clicker flap deflects via a small spring each time a segment boundary passes, with sensoryFeedback(.impact) per tick; eases to a wedge. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-flicks the wheel each cycle with a seeded angular velocity; it spins, the flap clacks past wedges, and it coasts to a stop before re-flicking.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onEnded computes angular velocity from recent samples; integrator decays rotation by friction; flap deflects + sensoryFeedback(.impact) on each segment crossing; snaps the pointer to the final wedge center.

name: Gravity Bin Toss
behavior: Fling chips toward a bin and they arc under gravity, bounce off the rim or each other, and pile up with stacking collisions while overflow chips spill and settle.
wow: Real projectile arcs plus chip-on-chip stacking make a pile that genuinely accumulates from your throws.
mechanism: DragGesture launch velocity feeds a lightweight TimelineView(.animation) physics step (gravity, restitution, circle-vs-circle and rim collisions) over a small array of chips rendered in Canvas; settled chips stack and overflow spills. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-tosses one chip per cycle into the bin; it arcs, bounces off the rim, and lands on the growing pile, resetting when the bin fills.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onEnded derives velocity from predictedEndTranslation and spawns a chip into the integrator; per-step gravity + pairwise collision resolution build the pile; sensoryFeedback(.impact) on rim/chip contacts.
risk: perf: cap chips (~20) and use simple circle collisions; full N² collision on many bodies will jank — verify on device.

name: Hold-to-Charge Burst
behavior: Press and hold to charge a core that pulses faster, swells, and accumulates orbiting energy rings; release fires a burst whose size scales with how long you held.
wow: The visible build-up of strain and orbiting energy makes the release feel earned and explosively proportional to your patience.
mechanism: A press timer (DragGesture(minimumDistance:0) onChanged/onEnded, or LongPressGesture) accumulates charge driving core scale + pulse rate + orbiting rings; release spawns a Canvas/TimelineView particle burst whose radius scales with charge. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.idle, .charging, .burst] auto-charges the core (swell + faster pulse + rings) then fires a particle burst and resets—looping charge cycle.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture(minimumDistance:0).onChanged starts a charge clock incrementing chargeLevel (clamped); core scale/pulse and orbit count follow it with escalating sensoryFeedback; onEnded fires a Canvas particle burst sized by chargeLevel + sensoryFeedback(.success).

name: Hold-Aim Trajectory Cannon
behavior: Press and drag back from a cannon to set power and angle, a dotted predicted arc updating live; release fires a ball that follows that exact arc and bounces on landing.
wow: The live predicted trajectory dots redraw as you aim, then the shot lands precisely on the previewed path.
mechanism: LongPressGesture.sequenced(before: DragGesture) sets power+angle from the pull-back vector; a dotted predicted arc is drawn by sampling the projectile equation each frame; release fires a TimelineView integrator that follows the same equation and bounces (restitution) on landing. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.aim, .fire, .reset] auto-pulls back (redrawing the dotted arc), fires the ball along the previewed path, and bounces it—looping aim-and-shoot.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture().sequenced(before: DragGesture()) maps -pull vector to power/angle; the dotted arc samples x(t),y(t) live; onEnded fires the ball through the same parametric path with a bounce on landing + sensoryFeedback(.impact).

name: Inertial Marble Tray
behavior: Fling a marble across a bordered tray and it coasts with friction, banks off the walls with energy loss, and slowly rolls to a stop, casting a moving contact shadow.
wow: Wall bounces lose just enough energy that the marble's decay feels physically correct, like a real ball on a tray.
mechanism: DragGesture velocity seeds a manual inertial integrator stepped by TimelineView(.animation): position += velocity*dt, velocity *= friction, walls reflect velocity with a restitution factor; a blurred ellipse tracks position as the contact shadow. iOS 17.
reference: UIKit Dynamics ball toss (system physics lineage)
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-launches the marble with a seeded velocity every loop; it banks off two walls, decays to rest, then re-launches—no finger needed.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onEnded reads predictedEndTranslation to derive launch velocity; TimelineView integrator carries momentum with wall reflection + friction; sensoryFeedback(.impact) on each wall bounce.

name: Jelly Drag Blob
behavior: Drag a soft blob and its trailing edge lags and stretches into a teardrop while the leading edge bulges, jiggling to a round rest shape when you stop.
wow: The blob's silhouette deforms with velocity so fast drags squash it thin and stops let it wobble back like gelatin.
mechanism: DragGesture velocity drives an Animatable Shape (Path with control points biased opposite the motion vector) that stretches into a teardrop; on stop the deform value relaxes via .interpolatingSpring(bounce:0.5) for the jiggle. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView drives a virtual velocity in a small orbit so the blob auto-stretches into a teardrop along its path and wobbles round when the loop pauses.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged feeds translation delta as velocity into the Shape's animatableData (stretch magnitude + direction); onEnded relaxes deform to 0 with a bouncy spring jiggle.

name: Long-Press Melt Button
behavior: Hold a button and it softens, drooping and sagging with gooey drips forming at the bottom edge that lengthen the longer you hold, snapping back solid on release.
wow: The button visibly liquefies and forms hanging droplets under sustained pressure, rewarding a long hold with surreal goo.
mechanism: A press clock (DragGesture(minimumDistance:0)) drives a melt value; the button is an Animatable Shape whose bottom edge sags and grows teardrop drips (Canvas alphaThreshold+blur fuses drips for goo); release springs the shape back solid. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.solid, .melting, .solid] auto-holds the melt: the bottom sags, drips lengthen, then snap back—looping liquefaction.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture(minimumDistance:0).onChanged accumulates hold time into meltLevel driving the Shape's sag + drip length; Canvas blur/threshold fuses drips; onEnded springs meltLevel→0 solid with a .bouncy spring.
risk: Goo via Canvas alphaThreshold+blur (no Metal); tune so drips read as liquid not blobs; cap drip count.

name: Ferrofluid Magnet Pull
behavior: Drag a magnet over a pool of dark fluid and spiky tendrils rise and lean toward it, merging and splitting as the magnet moves and slumping back flat when it leaves.
wow: The fluid grows reaching spikes that track the magnet, recreating the eerie crawl of real ferrofluid under a field.
mechanism: Canvas renders the pool with addFilter(.alphaThreshold) + .blur for a metaball look; spike blobs are placed along the surface, their height/lean computed from distance to the DragGesture magnet point; release relaxes heights with a spring. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView moves a virtual magnet point in a slow figure-eight; the metaball spikes auto-rise and lean toward it and slump where it left—living ferrofluid loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged sets the magnet location feeding each spike's height = falloff(distance) and lean toward the point; Canvas alphaThreshold+blur fuses neighbors; onEnded relaxes spikes flat with a spring.
risk: metaball needs Canvas alphaThreshold + blur (no Metal); tune threshold/blur and cap blob count — verify the gooey merge and frame rate on device.

name: Peel-Away Sticker
behavior: Drag a corner of a card and it peels up like a sticker with a curling lit underside and a soft drop shadow following the lifted edge, snapping flat or fully tearing free based on release velocity.
wow: The peeling corner shows realistic curl shading and a paper-back side, making a flat card feel like a physical decal.
mechanism: DragGesture on a corner drives a fold line; the lifted triangle is drawn with rotation3DEffect about the crease axis plus a LinearGradient highlight on its underside, with a shadow offset tracking the lifted edge; .spring snaps flat or releases past a velocity threshold. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView eases peel progress 0→0.6→0 so the corner lifts, the lit backside and shadow appear, and it lays back down—auto-curling forever.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged maps corner translation to peel angle and crease position; underside gradient + shadow follow the edge; onEnded uses predictedEndTranslation velocity to either spring flat or fling free with opacity fade.

name: Pinch-to-Collapse Stack
behavior: Pinching a photo grid closed makes every tile fly inward and fan-stack into a single tilted pile in the center; spreading fingers re-deals them back out across the grid with staggered timing.
wow: A whole grid collapses into a tactile shuffled deck and re-deals like cards, all continuously controlled by pinch scale.
mechanism: MagnifyGesture scale interpolates each tile between its grid frame and a center stack offset/rotation via per-index staggered progress; matchedGeometryEffect or manual offset+rotationEffect with .spring on release. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView(.animation) cycles a virtual pinch scale 1→0.2→1 every ~3s; a few tiles stagger-collapse into a tilted pile and re-deal so the tile reads as a self-shuffling deck.
interactiveSpec: MagnifyGesture().onChanged maps magnification to collapse progress 0–1; each tile lerps gridFrame→stackCenter with index-based rotation/offset; onEnded snaps collapsed or expanded with .spring(bounce:0.3).
risk: perf: cap tile count (~12–16) and precompute stack transforms; staggered springs on many views can drop frames.

name: Pinch Depth Diorama
behavior: Pinch on a layered scene to dolly the camera between parallax planes, foreground elements scaling and sliding faster than background while blur shifts to the focused depth.
wow: Pinch becomes a depth slider through stacked planes, creating a dolly-zoom diorama feel from flat layers.
mechanism: MagnifyGesture maps to a virtual camera depth; each ZStack layer applies scaleEffect and offset multiplied by its depth coefficient, with .blur whose radius is the distance from the focused plane; .spring settles on release. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView eases camera depth front→back→front; planes parallax-scale at different rates and the in-focus plane sharpens while others blur—auto dolly loop.
interactiveSpec: MagnifyGesture().onChanged maps magnification to focusDepth; per-layer scale/offset = depthCoeff*delta and blur = |layerDepth - focusDepth|; onEnded snaps to the nearest plane with .spring.
risk: perf: heavy .blur on several large layers is costly — limit to ~4 planes and modest blur radii; verify on device.

name: Pinch Origami Fold
behavior: Pinch a flat square and it folds along crease lines through intermediate origami states into a standing crane, each fold catching directional light, unfolding as you spread.
wow: Continuous pinch scrubs through a real folding sequence with shaded facets, like animating paper into a sculpture by hand.
mechanism: MagnifyGesture maps to a 0–1 fold timeline; facets are triangle Paths whose vertices interpolate between keyframed fold states (KeyframeAnimator/manual lerp) with rotation3DEffect, each facet's LinearGradient brightness keyed to its fold normal for directional light. iOS 18 per the stable field; the underlying Path + rotation3DEffect + KeyframeAnimator approach also runs on iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView scrubs the fold timeline 0→1→0 so the flat square auto-folds into the crane and unfolds, facets catching light as they rotate—hands-free origami.
interactiveSpec: MagnifyGesture().onChanged maps magnification to foldProgress; each facet interpolates its keyframed vertex set and rotation3DEffect, gradient brightness from facet angle; onEnded snaps to nearest keyframe state with .spring.
risk: Most complex item: many interpolated facets with rotation3DEffect + per-facet shading. Keep facet count bounded and precompute keyframe vertex sets; verify the fold reads cleanly on device.

name: Pinch-Clamp Crop Frame
behavior: Pinch within an image and a crop frame resists past content edges with rubber-band overscroll, the dimmed outside region springing taut when you let go beyond bounds.
wow: Boundary rubber-banding makes the crop frame feel anchored to the photo's edges with believable give and snap-back.
mechanism: MagnifyGesture (with focal anchor) + DragGesture pan adjust scale/offset; an asymptotic rubber-band function applies past-edge resistance and a dimmed mask covers the outside; onEnded clamps back into bounds with .spring. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView eases a virtual pinch scale past the edge and back 1→1.4→1; the frame overscrolls with rubber-band give, dimming the outside, then snaps taut to bounds—auto crop loop.
interactiveSpec: MagnifyGesture().onChanged scales about the focal anchor with rubber-band resistance beyond content bounds; DragGesture pans with the same clamp; onEnded springs scale/offset back inside bounds + sensoryFeedback(.impact) on the snap.

name: Slingshot Launcher
behavior: Pull a projectile back against a stretching elastic band that thins and tints under tension, then release to fling it forward along the loaded vector while the band recoils with a twang wobble.
wow: The band visibly thins and changes color with tension and snaps back oscillating, turning a button into a real slingshot.
mechanism: DragGesture sets a pull vector; two Path lines from fixed anchors to the projectile draw the band, its lineWidth and color interpolated by tension magnitude; release fires along the vector and the band recoils with .interpolatingSpring oscillation. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.idle, .draw, .release] auto-pulls the band back (thinning/tinting) and snaps it forward with a twang wobble, looping on its own.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged sets pull point and band thickness/hue from -translation magnitude; onEnded fires the projectile along the released vector with a TimelineView coast and a damped spring band recoil + sensoryFeedback(.impact).

name: Twist-to-Rewind Clock
behavior: Rotate a clock face backward and the hands wind in reverse while a ghosted scene behind it visually un-happens, fast-forwarding when you twist the other way.
wow: Scrubbing time with a twist visibly rewinds and replays a small scene, turning rotation into a time machine.
mechanism: RotateGesture maps rotation to a time scrubber t; clock hands rotationEffect track t and a KeyframeAnimator/manual interpolation plays a small scene's state backward/forward along t; sensoryFeedback(.selection) per hour tick. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView sweeps t forward then backward; the hands wind and the ghosted scene plays and un-plays—auto time-scrub loop.
interactiveSpec: RotateGesture().onChanged maps cumulative rotation to time t (clamped); hands rotate to t and the scene's keyframed state samples at t in either direction; sensoryFeedback(.selection, trigger: hour); onEnded eases to nearest hour.

name: Combination Safe Dial
behavior: Rotate a numbered safe dial back and forth; correct numbers in sequence light up and on the final entry the dial spins free, a bolt retracts, and the door eases open.
wow: The mechanical reward of the bolt thunking back and the door swinging makes solving the combo deeply satisfying.
mechanism: RotateGesture (or DragGesture + atan2) drives the dial's rotationEffect with sensoryFeedback(.selection) per number; a small state machine tracks the correct sequence; on solve a bolt offset retracts and the door swings via rotation3DEffect with .spring. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator scripts the full combo: dial turns to each number (lighting up), then the bolt retracts and door eases open, then re-closes—auto solve loop.
interactiveSpec: RotateGesture().onChanged turns the dial and snaps to integer numbers with sensoryFeedback(.selection); the sequence matcher advances on direction reversals; final match springs the bolt back + rotation3DEffect door swing + sensoryFeedback(.success).

name: Tactile Detent Dial
behavior: Twist a knurled dial with a rotation gesture and it clicks through evenly spaced detents, with the needle slightly overshooting each notch and a tick mark flashing as it passes.
wow: Each detent has a micro-overshoot and haptic tick, recreating the satisfying notchy resistance of a real hardware knob.
mechanism: RotateGesture (or DragGesture + atan2) drives rotationEffect; angle snaps to nearest detent with a .spring(bounce:0.4) micro-overshoot, and sensoryFeedback(.impact, trigger: detentIndex) fires plus a tick flash on each notch crossing. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView sweeps the dial angle through several detents 0→full→0; each notch crossing flashes its tick and the needle micro-overshoots so the knob auto-clicks around.
interactiveSpec: RotateGesture().onChanged adds rotation.radians to angle; onEnded snaps to nearest detent with spring overshoot; sensoryFeedback(.impact, trigger: nearestDetent) gives the click on every notch.

name: Meshed Gear Train
behavior: Rotate a drive gear and a train of meshed gears turns in correct alternating directions at ratio-scaled speeds, with a brief backlash jiggle when you reverse direction.
wow: Tooth-accurate ratios and reversal backlash make the whole mechanism feel like a real geartrain you are cranking.
mechanism: RotateGesture (or atan2 DragGesture) drives the drive gear's rotationEffect; each downstream gear's angle = driveAngle * (-teethRatio); a tiny lag term adds backlash jiggle on direction reversal via .interpolatingSpring; gears drawn as toothed Shapes. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView spins the drive gear forward, pauses, reverses; the meshed train turns at ratio speeds with a backlash jiggle on each reversal—auto cranking loop.
interactiveSpec: RotateGesture().onChanged sets driveAngle; downstream gears compute angle via gear-ratio multipliers each frame; a small spring-lag on reversal gives backlash; sensoryFeedback(.selection) per tooth-mesh tick.
risk: Tooth alignment must be precomputed so meshing looks correct; ratio errors read as gears slipping — verify visually.

name: Vinyl Scrub Platter
behavior: Spin a record platter with a rotate gesture; it keeps momentum and slows by friction, and dragging against the spin scrubs it back with a pitch-bent wobble of the label art.
wow: Momentum plus back-cue scrubbing recreates the hands-on feel of a DJ nudging a turntable.
mechanism: RotateGesture (or atan2 DragGesture) sets angular velocity; a TimelineView friction integrator carries momentum on rotationEffect; scrubbing against the spin applies a brief scale/blur wobble to the label for the pitch-bend feel. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-spins the platter up, lets friction coast it down, then nudges it backward with a label wobble—looping scratch demo, no finger.
interactiveSpec: RotateGesture().onChanged adds to angular velocity; integrator decays by friction each frame; back-cue when finger opposes spin applies a label scale/opacity wobble; sensoryFeedback(.selection) per revolution tick.

name: Rubber-Band Sheet Morph
behavior: A bottom sheet you drag upward stretches past its rest height with rubber-band resistance, and its contents re-flow from a single compact summary row into a full multi-column layout as it rises, then snaps back with an overshoot spring.
wow: The content literally restructures itself proportionally to drag distance, so the panel feels like it is physically inflating rather than just sliding.
mechanism: DragGesture height with an asymptotic rubber-band clamp past max; a normalized progress (GeometryReader height vs. detents) drives a layout crossfade from compact row to grid, releasing on .interpolatingSpring/.snappy. iOS 17.
reference: Apple Maps / Music bottom sheet detents
previewLoop: TimelineView(.animation) eases progress 0→1→0 every ~3.5s, raising the sheet and morphing summary-row→two-column content in lockstep so the tile inflates and deflates on its own.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().updating height with rubber-band resistance beyond top detent; onEnded snaps to nearest detent via predictedEndTranslation; progress feeds opacity/scale of the two layouts and a sensoryFeedback(.impact) on each detent crossing.

name: Stage Curtain Part
behavior: Swipe outward and two heavy fabric curtains part with rippling folds and weighted drag, gathering at the edges with bunched pleats to reveal the content stage.
wow: The curtains bunch and ripple with cloth weight as they gather, giving a theatrical reveal that lags realistically behind your swipe.
mechanism: DragGesture sets a part-progress lagged behind the finger; each curtain is an Animatable Shape with a sine-fold edge whose amplitude/bunching increases as it gathers to the side; .spring with heavier damping conveys cloth weight. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView eases part-progress 0→1→0; the two curtains gather to the edges with rippling pleats revealing the stage, then close again—auto theatrical loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged maps |translation.width| to part-progress with a lag filter; each curtain Shape's fold amplitude + width follow it; onEnded springs fully open/closed with a weighted .spring(duration:0.6, bounce:0.15).

name: Domino Swipe Cascade
behavior: Swipe across a row of standing tiles and they topple in sequence, each knocking the next with a slight delay and angular fall, the last one triggering an action.
wow: A chain-reaction topple propagates from your swipe at domino speed, turning a gesture into a kinetic sequence.
mechanism: DragGesture x-position is the topple front; each tile's fall angle (rotation3DEffect about its base edge) triggers with a per-index delay once the front passes, via staggered .spring; the final tile fires the action + sensoryFeedback(.success). iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView sweeps the topple front across the row 0→1→0; tiles fall in sequence with staggered delays and stand back up—auto domino cascade.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged advances the front by location.x; each tile past the front animates rotation3DEffect to fallen with index-delayed .spring + sensoryFeedback(.impact) per topple; the last tile triggers the action.

name: Elastic Action Reveal
behavior: Swiping a list row left stretches a single action button that elongates elastically; past a threshold it pops into a wide pill that springs to full width and auto-fires with haptic confirmation.
wow: The action button is a stretchy blob that wobbles under your finger, then snaps confidently when committed rather than appearing instantly.
mechanism: DragGesture x-translation drives an animatable Capsule width with diminishing-return resistance; crossing a threshold triggers a .bouncy spring to full width plus sensoryFeedback(.success). iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.rest, .stretch, .commit] auto-drives the row offset and button width 0→1→0 so the pill stretches, pops wide, and recoils without a finger.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged maps -translation.width to button width via rubber-band curve; onEnded if past threshold springs to full + sensoryFeedback(.success) and fires, else springs back to rest.

name: Swipe Deck Shuffle
behavior: Swipe the top card off a fanned stack and the cards beneath glide up and re-fan with slight rotation jitter, the flung card tumbling away with spin proportional to swipe speed.
wow: The whole stack re-fans organically with per-card jitter, so it feels like a living hand of cards rather than a queue.
mechanism: DragGesture translation+velocity rotates and offsets the top card (rotationEffect anchored low) and flings it off past threshold; remaining cards interpolate to new fan offsets/rotations with per-index seeded jitter via .spring. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-flings the top card off each cycle with spin; the deck re-fans with jittered offsets, then the card returns to the back—self-shuffling stack.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged sets top card offset + rotation; onEnded if velocity/translation past threshold flings it along the vector with spin, else springs back; deck advances and re-fans with .spring + sensoryFeedback(.impact) on commit.

name: Swipe-Stamp Impress
behavior: Drag a rubber stamp down onto a surface and it presses in with a squash, leaving an ink impression that blooms outward with slight bleed where pressure was highest.
wow: The ink mark blooms and bleeds from the contact point, making the stamp feel pressed rather than pasted.
mechanism: DragGesture downward motion scales the stamp with a squash (scaleEffect anchored bottom) on contact; the impression reveals via a mask that blooms outward (.spring on a circular reveal) with a feathered edge for ink bleed; stamp lifts back. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.up, .pressed, .up] auto-drops the stamp, squashes on contact, blooms the inked impression, then lifts—looping stamping.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged maps downward translation to approach + squash; on contact a bloom mask grows the ink with feathered bleed + sensoryFeedback(.impact); onEnded lifts the stamp leaving the impression.

name: Tear-Off Receipt
behavior: Drag down on a perforated stub and it tears along a jagged dotted line with a ragged fiber edge, the torn piece falling away with a flutter as the remainder springs up.
wow: The rip follows a procedurally ragged perforation and the freed piece flutters as it falls, mimicking real paper tearing.
mechanism: DragGesture progress reveals a jagged tear via an Animatable Shape whose edge path uses a seeded random sawtooth keyed to progress (clip mask between halves); past threshold the freed piece detaches and flutters with rotation3DEffect + offset, remainder springs up. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.intact, .tearing, .gone] auto-runs the rip down the perforation, flutters the stub away, and resets the receipt—looping tear.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged maps translation.height to tear progress driving the jagged clip path with sensoryFeedback(.selection) ticks along the perforation; onEnded past threshold detaches + flutters the piece, else springs back together.
risk: Generate the ragged edge once with a seeded RNG so the jag is stable across frames; animate only the cut position.

name: Boomerang Toss
behavior: Fling an object and it arcs out, decelerates, curves back along a banking return path, and lands back in the launch slot with a satisfying catch bounce.
wow: The object reliably loops home on a curved return arc, so every throw resolves like a caught boomerang.
mechanism: DragGesture launch velocity parameterizes a TimelineView-driven curved return path (outbound vector + centripetal curl back to origin); the object follows the path with banking rotationEffect and a catch .spring squash on arrival. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-flings the object each cycle; it arcs out, curves back along the banking return, and lands in the slot with a catch bounce before re-launching.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onEnded reads velocity from predictedEndTranslation to set the outbound vector and return curvature; TimelineView animates along the computed return arc with banking rotation; catch springs into the slot + sensoryFeedback(.impact).

name: Two-Finger Shear Card
behavior: Drag two fingers in opposite directions across a card to shear it into a parallelogram with a glossy skew highlight, releasing to spring back to a true rectangle with a wobble.
wow: The card deforms along your two-finger vector like flexible glass, then snaps square with an elastic settle.
mechanism: Two simultaneous DragGestures (top/bottom halves) or a custom two-touch handler set a shear amount applied via a ProjectionTransform/.transformEffect(CGAffineTransform shear); a moving LinearGradient highlight tracks the skew; release runs .interpolatingSpring(bounce:0.45) back to identity. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.rect, .sheared, .rect] auto-skews the card into a parallelogram with the gloss sweep, then wobbles square—no finger.
interactiveSpec: SimultaneousGesture of two DragGestures gives opposing offsets; their difference sets the shear coefficient in a CGAffineTransform applied with .transformEffect; onEnded springs back with elastic wobble + sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: Affine shear only (no Metal distortion); two-finger capture on a small view can conflict with system gestures — verify hit-testing on device.

name: Two-Finger Rip Seam
behavior: Place two fingers and pull apart to rip a panel along a stretching seam that whitens under stress before splitting into two ragged halves that recoil.
wow: The material stretches and stress-whitens before tearing exactly between your fingers, so you feel the moment it gives.
mechanism: Two simultaneous DragGestures (MagnifyGesture spread also works) set the pull separation; the seam is an Animatable Shape that stretches and brightens (stress-whitening via overlay opacity) until past threshold it splits into two ragged Paths that recoil with .interpolatingSpring. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator over [.sealed, .stressed, .torn] auto-pulls the seam: it stretches and whitens, snaps into two ragged halves that recoil, then re-seals—looping rip.
interactiveSpec: SimultaneousGesture/two DragGestures give the separation; seam Shape stretches and a white stress overlay fades in with separation; onEnded past threshold splits into two ragged halves recoiling apart + sensoryFeedback(.success), else springs back sealed.
risk: Seeded ragged edge generated once for stability; two-finger capture on a small view can clash with system gestures — verify hit-testing.

name: Zipper Unseal
behavior: Drag a zipper pull downward and interlocking teeth separate one pair at a time along the track, splitting a sealed panel open to reveal content beneath with a faint fabric flex.
wow: Teeth disengage sequentially right at the pull head, so the seam opens with believable mechanical zipper motion.
mechanism: DragGesture y-position is the unzip front; teeth (Path/Shape rows) below the front interlock, above it splay left/right with offset+rotation; the two fabric panels animate their split gap and a subtle flex curve via an Animatable Shape. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView drives the pull head 0→bottom→0; teeth disengage sequentially at the moving front and the panels part, then re-seal, looping with a faint fabric flex.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture().onChanged sets unzipFront = clamp(location.y); teeth above front splay via interpolation, panels open to gap; sensoryFeedback(.selection, trigger: teethPassed) ticks per tooth; onEnded springs open or re-sealed.
risk: perf: keep teeth count modest (~24) and drive them from one progress value rather than per-tooth gestures.

name: Bubble Wrap Pop
behavior: A grid of glossy bubbles deflates one-by-one on tap with a quick squish-and-flatten, an auto-loop randomly popping and re-inflating them.
wow: It is irresistible tactile bubble wrap: each bubble caves in with a wet squish you can almost hear.
mechanism: LazyVGrid of glossy bubbles (Circle + RadialGradient highlight); tap sets a per-cell popped flag animating a quick scaleEffect squish-and-flatten with .spring; an idle Timer randomly pops/re-inflates cells. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A Timer (or TimelineView) randomly deflates and re-inflates bubbles across the grid so the tile self-pops continuously.
interactiveSpec: Per-bubble tap toggles its popped state with the squish spring and sensoryFeedback(.impact); re-inflates after a delay.

name: Copy Shred-Reform
behavior: On copy the label splits into vertical strips that shuffle and reassemble into the word Copied with a checkmark sliding in.
wow: The label shreds into ribbons that reshuffle into Copied, a paper-shredder-in-reverse confirmation.
mechanism: The label is sliced into vertical strips (ForEach of clipped Text columns via mask offsets); strips shuffle on staggered .spring offsets and reassemble into 'Copied', with a checkmark sliding in via transition. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator triggers on a timer; strips scatter/shuffle and reform into Copied with the tick, then reset, looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap triggers the shred-and-reform.
risk: low — per-strip masking math; keep strip count modest (~8-12)

name: Copy Vanish Confirm
behavior: On copy, the text duplicates, lifts off, and slides into a clipboard glyph that briefly bulges to swallow it, then shows a checkmark.
wow: You literally watch the text fly off the row and get gulped into the clipboard before the confirm tick.
mechanism: On tap a duplicate Text lifts (offset+scale) and travels via an animatable bezier offset into a clipboard SF Symbol that scaleEffect-bulges to swallow it, then a checkmark crossfades via contentTransition. matchedGeometryEffect optional. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator triggers copy on a timer; the ghost text flies into the clipboard glyph which bulges, then the tick shows, looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap fires the copy flight and clipboard swallow.

name: Day-Night Toggle
behavior: A switch morphs a sun into a crescent moon by sliding a shadow disc across it while tiny stars fade in and the track gradient shifts from sky-blue to indigo.
wow: One flick turns a sunny sky into a starry night via a crescent eclipse, with no asset swap, all drawn shapes.
mechanism: ZStack of drawn shapes: a sun Circle with a second Circle offset across it to carve the crescent (overlay with blendMode(.destinationOut) in a compositingGroup), tiny star Circles fade via opacity, track uses an animated LinearGradient sky->indigo. All interpolated on isOn with .easeInOut. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator flips isOn on a timer; the shadow disc slides across the sun, stars cross-fade, and the gradient shifts automatically in the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles isOn driving the same crescent slide and gradient shift.
risk: low — crescent needs compositingGroup()+blendMode(.destinationOut); verify the mask renders on device

name: Dog-Ear Bookmark
behavior: Tapping a card folds its top corner down into a triangular dog-ear with a soft drop shadow, peeling back up when untapped.
wow: Saving feels like physically folding a page corner, complete with the shadow of the lifted flap.
mechanism: A triangular corner Path with an Animatable fold progress; the folded flap is a second triangle with a soft drop shadow and a darker underside fill, rotated/scaled via the same progress on .spring(bounce:0.3). iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator toggles isSaved on a timer; the corner folds down and peels back up automatically in the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles isSaved driving the same fold.

name: Elastic Segmented Control
behavior: The selection pill stretches toward the tapped segment, overshooting and squashing on arrival while the active label crossfades weight.
wow: The selector pill rubber-bands between segments, momentarily stretching across the gap like elastic.
mechanism: A selection pill positioned with matchedGeometryEffect across segments; on tap it stretches by widening a Capsule (scaleEffect x>1 mid-travel) then squashes on arrival via .spring(bounce:0.5); active label crossfades fontWeight via contentTransition. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator cycles the selected index on a timer; the pill rubber-bands between segments with the stretch-and-squash automatically.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus tapping a segment animates the pill to it with the elastic stretch and label weight crossfade.

name: Emoji Fountain
behavior: Holding a button launches a continuous arcing fountain of emoji via Canvas, each with gravity, spin, and fade, the stream thickening the longer you hold.
wow: Press and hold to spray a physics-driven geyser of spinning emoji that intensifies with your hold.
mechanism: LongPressGesture/press state gates a TimelineView(.animation)+Canvas particle system; each emoji gets velocity, gravity, spin, and fade; emission rate ramps with hold duration. Drawn with context.draw(Text(...)) in Canvas. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView auto-pulses an emitter on/off; a steady arcing fountain of spinning emoji plays continuously in the tile without a press.
interactiveSpec: A press-and-hold (DragGesture(minimumDistance:0) or LongPressGesture) sets isEmitting=true and ramps spawn rate by elapsed hold; release stops emission and lets in-flight particles fall.
risk: perf: cap active particles (~150) and cull off-screen; one Canvas, no per-emoji views

name: Folding Star Favorite
behavior: A star favorite folds origami-style: its five points fold inward to a flat disc when off and unfold with a springy snap when favorited.
wow: The star literally origami-folds its points shut and unfolds them with a paper-crease snap.
mechanism: A five-point star whose points are Animatable Path segments folding inward to a flat disc by a progress value; unfold uses .spring(bounce:0.5) with directional fold-shadow gradients per point. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator toggles favorited on a timer; the star points fold shut to a disc and unfold with the springy snap, looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles favorited driving the origami fold/unfold with sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: animatable star Path must keep equal point counts folded vs unfolded; verify interpolation

name: Jelly Count Badge
behavior: A notification badge gains a number that drops in from above, squashing the badge into a wobbly jelly blob that wobbles back to a circle.
wow: The badge reacts to each new count with a juicy gelatin wobble instead of a static increment.
mechanism: A Circle badge with the number entering via .transition(.move(.top).combined(with:.opacity)) and contentTransition(.numericText); the badge squashes through scaleEffect(x:y:) on a .spring(bounce:0.6) jelly wobble. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator increments the count on a timer; each new number drops in and the badge does its gelatin squash-and-settle automatically.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop.

name: Knurled Stepper
behavior: A plus/minus stepper where the number sits on a knurled wheel that rotates one notch per tap with a detent bounce and a brief motion-blur smear.
wow: Incrementing feels like clicking a machined metal thumbwheel, each step landing on a notched detent.
mechanism: The number sits on a knurled wheel (repeated thin Rectangles around an arc) that rotates one notch per +/- tap via rotationEffect on a .spring(bounce:0.4) detent; brief motion-blur is a faded duplicate or .blur during the step. Drag also spins it. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator advances the value on a timer; the wheel clicks one notch each step with the detent bounce and smear, looping up/down.
interactiveSpec: +/- buttons increment with the one-notch detent spring; DragGesture vertically scrubs the wheel with momentum, snapping to the nearest notch. sensoryFeedback(.selection) per detent.

name: Snap-Together Choice Chips
behavior: Selectable chips drift slightly apart at rest; the chosen chip snaps to a magnetic notch with a click-in scale pop while siblings recoil.
wow: Choosing a chip clicks it into a magnetic slot while neighbors flinch back, like puzzle pieces settling.
mechanism: Chips rest with small randomized offsets (idle drift); the chosen chip animates to a fixed notch position with a .spring(bounce:0.5) click-in scale pop while siblings recoil via a brief offset spring. sensoryFeedback(.selection). iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator cycles the selection on a timer; the picked chip clicks into its notch and neighbors flinch, looping through chips.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus tapping a chip snaps it to its notch with the scale pop while siblings recoil.

name: Magnetic Snap Toggle
behavior: The toggle knob is dragged and, on release, accelerates with overshoot toward the nearest end as if pulled by a magnet, the track color sweeping behind it.
wow: The knob visibly fights you near the midpoint then yanks itself home with a magnetic snap that feels physically real.
mechanism: DragGesture moves the knob; on .onEnded a spring(response:0.35,dampingFraction:0.6) with overshoot drives it to the nearest end. Track fill animates via a clipped Capsule width tied to knob offset; sensoryFeedback(.selection) fires at the snap. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator cycles a normalized progress 0->1->0; the knob lerps across the track and the spring snap is faked by an easeOut-then-overshoot keyframe so the tile auto-shows the magnetic yank untouched.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture(minimumDistance:0) updates knob x clamped to track; .onEnded picks nearest end by midpoint and animates with .spring(bounce:0.4), plus a tap toggles directly. sensoryFeedback(.impact) on land.

name: Mood-Morph Rating
behavior: A single face icon continuously morphs its mouth path from frown to grin as you drag the rating, eyes widening at the top score.
wow: One emoji face smoothly bends its smile to match your rating in real time instead of five static stars.
mechanism: A single face whose mouth is an Animatable Path: control points of a quadratic curve interpolate frown->grin by a rating value; eyes scaleEffect at the top score. DragGesture drives the value, .spring settle. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator sweeps the rating 0->1->0; the mouth path continuously morphs frown to grin and back, eyes widen at peak, self-looping.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture x maps to a 0..1 rating that drives the mouth Path's animatableData live; release snaps to nearest notch with a small spring.
risk: animatable mouth Path needs matching point counts across states; verify morph stays smooth

name: Mute Wave Morph
behavior: Tapping mute collapses three animated sound waves inward into the speaker and slashes a line across it; unmuting springs the waves back out.
wow: The sound waves retract into the speaker as the mute slash draws on, a single fluid path morph.
mechanism: Three sound-wave arcs (Shape .trim) retract into the speaker on mute while a slash Path strokes on via trim; the speaker glyph can use contentTransition.symbolEffect(.replace), all interpolated on isMuted with .spring. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator toggles isMuted on a timer; waves collapse in and the slash draws, then springs back out, looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles isMuted driving the wave retract and slash stroke.

name: Orbit Badge
behavior: When a count crosses a threshold the badge spawns a tiny dot that orbits it once with a comet trail before merging back in.
wow: A milestone count is celebrated by a small satellite looping the badge with a trailing comet streak.
mechanism: On threshold crossing, a small Circle follows an angular path (rotationEffect of an offset dot, or TimelineView angle) once around the badge while a Canvas/fading trail draws the comet streak, then it merges via scale/opacity. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator triggers the milestone on a timer; the satellite dot loops the badge once with its comet trail and merges back, repeating.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop.
risk: low — keep the comet trail to a short Canvas point buffer

name: OTP Cascade Entry
behavior: As each code digit is entered its box flips up to reveal the number with a staggered domino delay; a wrong code shakes the whole row in unison.
wow: Digits flip into place like a falling-domino split-flap board, and a mistake shudders the entire row.
mechanism: Each digit box flips up via rotation3DEffect(axis:.x) on entry with a per-box .delay (domino stagger) and contentTransition for the number; a wrong code runs a shared shake via a KeyframeAnimator/phaseAnimator x-offset on the whole row. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator auto-types digits one-by-one with the staggered flip, then triggers the failure shake and clears, looping the fill->shake cycle.
interactiveSpec: Keyboard/text input fills boxes; each new digit triggers its flip-up with stagger; on validation failure the row plays the shake KeyframeAnimator and clears. Tap focuses the field.

name: Particle Assemble Check
behavior: Dozens of scattered dots fly inward via Canvas and lock into the silhouette of a checkmark, the last few settling with a springy jitter.
wow: The checkmark materializes out of a swarm of converging particles rather than being drawn.
mechanism: TimelineView(.animation) + Canvas: each particle lerps from a scattered start to a target sampled along the checkmark path, last few using a spring-eased t for jitter. Targets precomputed from Path points. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView drives a normalized t 0->1->reset; particles converge into the check silhouette then scatter back out, self-looping in the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop.
risk: perf: cap particle count (~40-60) and reuse one Canvas; avoid per-particle SwiftUI views

name: Password Reveal Flip
behavior: Tapping the eye toggles each masked dot flipping in sequence to reveal its character, with the eye lid blinking open or shut.
wow: The dots flip over one-by-one into letters as a tiny eyelid blinks, instead of an instant unmask.
mechanism: Tapping the eye toggles each dot flipping in sequence via rotation3DEffect(axis:.y) with a per-index .delay swapping dot->character at the 90 degree midpoint; the eye is an SF Symbol using contentTransition.symbolEffect(.replace) for the lid blink. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator toggles revealed on a timer; dots flip to letters in sequence as the eye lid blinks, then flip back, looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus tapping the eye toggles revealed which drives the staggered dot flips and lid blink.

name: PIN Shake-to-Success
behavior: PIN dots fill as you type; on success they merge into a single dot that expands into a checkmark, on failure they scatter and shake.
wow: Correct PIN dots coalesce into one growing check while a wrong one explodes the dots apart in a shudder.
mechanism: PIN dots fill as Circles; on success they converge (matchedGeometryEffect/offset to center) and merge into one dot that scales into a check Path (trim); on failure a KeyframeAnimator x-offset shake scatters them. sensoryFeedback(.success/.error). iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator auto-fills the dots then alternates success (merge->check) and failure (scatter+shake) on a loop in the tile.
interactiveSpec: Tap/keypad fills dots; on correct PIN the dots merge-and-grow into the check; on wrong PIN the row runs the shake KeyframeAnimator and resets.

name: Popcorn Emoji Burst
behavior: Tapping pops a cluster of emoji that fire upward at staggered times with slight horizontal drift, each rotating and shrinking like popping kernels.
wow: Emoji burst in irregular staggered pops like popcorn rather than a uniform confetti spray.
mechanism: On tap a TimelineView+Canvas (or staggered .spring views) fires a cluster upward at randomized start delays with horizontal drift, each rotating and shrinking via gravity-eased t. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator triggers a burst on a timer; the staggered irregular pops fire and fade, then repeat automatically.
interactiveSpec: SpatialTapGesture spawns the cluster from the tap point with per-particle random delay/drift/rotation; sensoryFeedback(.impact) on tap.

name: Pressure Pop
behavior: A long-press fills a radial pressure ring that, on completion, releases with a sudden scale-pop and outward shockwave puff.
wow: You feel the build-up: a charging ring tensions up then releases with a satisfying pop and air-puff.
mechanism: LongPressGesture progress fills a radial ring via Circle .trim(to:progress); on completion a KeyframeAnimator runs independent scale-pop and an expanding shockwave ring (scale up + opacity down). sensoryFeedback(.impact) on pop. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator/TimelineView auto-fills the ring 0->1 then plays the pop+shockwave and resets, self-looping in the tile.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture(minimumDuration:0.6) drives ring fill via its progress; on .onEnded(perform completion) the KeyframeAnimator pop+shockwave fires.

name: Punch-Out Check
behavior: A filled chip flips and a checkmark appears as negative space punched through it, light leaking through the cut edges as the chip settles.
wow: The check is not drawn but stamped out as a hole, reading like a physical hole-punch through a card.
mechanism: A filled RoundedRectangle chip with a check Path drawn via blendMode(.destinationOut) inside compositingGroup() so the check is negative space; a 3D flip uses rotation3DEffect(.degrees, axis:.y) and edge light is a gradient stroke leaking through. .spring settle. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator cycles the flip 0->180->0 on a timer so the chip turns and the punched check appears and hides automatically.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles the flip that reveals/hides the punch-out.
risk: low — destinationOut punch-out requires compositingGroup(); confirm on device

name: Ink-Fill Radio
behavior: Selecting a radio option drops a spreading ink blot that fills the circle from the touch point, deselecting the previous one which drains away.
wow: Selection spreads like a drop of ink soaking into paper, with the old choice draining out in tandem.
mechanism: Selecting drops an expanding ink Circle clipped to the radio (scaleEffect 0->1 with .easeOut) spreading from the touch point; the previously selected one drains (scale->0 + opacity). matchedGeometryEffect can carry the active dot. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator cycles the selected option on a timer; ink blooms into the new radio while the old drains, looping through choices.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus tapping an option blooms ink from the tap point and drains the prior selection.

name: Reaction Tray Roll
behavior: A long-press opens a row of emoji that arc up and scale in sequence; sliding across magnifies the hovered one like a fisheye dock.
wow: The reaction picker bows up into a fisheye-magnified dock that swells the emoji under your thumb.
mechanism: LongPressGesture opens the tray; emoji arc up and scale in with a staggered .spring sequence (per-index delay). A trailing DragGesture computes distance from each item to the touch x and applies a fisheye scale falloff. iOS 17.
reference: iMessage reaction tray / macOS Dock magnification
previewLoop: TimelineView sweeps a virtual cursor x across the row 0->1->0; items arc in once then the fisheye magnification rides back and forth automatically.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture.sequenced(before: DragGesture) — long-press opens with staggered springs; drag x feeds the per-item distance->scale fisheye; release commits the hovered emoji.

name: Ribbon Drop Bookmark
behavior: A ribbon unfurls downward from the top edge with a pendulum swing and a swallowtail-notched bottom, settling with a gentle sway when saved.
wow: A cloth ribbon physically drops and sways into place like a real bookmark tucked into a journal.
mechanism: A ribbon Rectangle with a swallowtail notch Path drops from the top edge using offset + an anchored rotationEffect for the pendulum, settling with .spring(response:0.5,dampingFraction:0.55) so it sways. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator toggles isSaved on a timer; the ribbon unfurls down with a pendulum swing and settles, then retracts, looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles isSaved triggering the drop-and-sway.

name: Rolodex Quantity
behavior: A quantity picker where flicking spins a vertical cylinder of numbers with momentum and perspective curl, decelerating to snap a value to center.
wow: Numbers ride a curved 3D cylinder you can fling and watch coast to a stop, not a flat picker wheel.
mechanism: A vertical cylinder of numbers: each row offset+rotation3DEffect(axis:.x) and opacity by its angular distance from center to fake the curl. DragGesture velocity feeds a friction-decay TimelineView that snaps the nearest value to center. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView spins the cylinder with decaying velocity on a loop; numbers curl past with perspective and coast to a snapped value, repeating.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture velocity on .onEnded seeds a friction-decayed offset (TimelineView integrator); it decelerates and snaps the nearest value to center with .spring; sensoryFeedback(.selection) per crossing.
risk: low — perspective via rotation3DEffect per row; keep visible row count small

name: Waveform Scrubber
behavior: Dragging a scrubber across an audio waveform parts the bars around the playhead and ripples a height-wave outward from the touch point.
wow: The waveform bars bulge and part around your finger as if the sound itself reacts to the touch.
mechanism: A Canvas (or HStack of Capsule bars) draws the waveform; a DragGesture playhead x parts neighboring bars (offset by distance falloff) and adds a height bump that ripples outward. .spring settle. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView sweeps the playhead x 0->1->0; bars part and the height-wave ripples around the moving playhead automatically.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture x sets the playhead; per-bar offset/height computed from distance to playhead with a falloff; sensoryFeedback(.selection) on tick. Release leaves the playhead.
risk: perf: many bars updating per frame — keep bar count bounded and verify smoothness on device

name: Slide to Confirm
behavior: A handle is dragged along a track that fills behind it with a liquid sheen; reaching the end snaps it shut and morphs the arrow into a checkmark.
wow: A deliberate slide-to-unlock confirm with a filling sheen and an arrow that flips to a tick at the end.
mechanism: DragGesture moves a handle clamped along a track; track fills behind it with a moving LinearGradient sheen (phaseAnimator offset). At the end it snaps shut and the arrow morphs to a check via contentTransition.symbolEffect(.replace). sensoryFeedback(.success). iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator ramps progress 0->1->reset; the handle slides, sheen fills, and the arrow flips to a checkmark, then resets, looping.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture drags the handle clamped to [0,trackWidth]; past ~0.9 it locks with a spring and morphs arrow->check; below threshold it springs back.

name: Staggered Star Rating
behavior: Dragging across stars fills them left-to-right; each newly filled star pops with a staggered spring and the selected count tints warmer as it rises.
wow: Stars ignite in a rippling cascade under your finger, the warmth of the row growing with the score.
mechanism: DragGesture x maps to a rating 0..5; each filled star pops via a per-index .spring with a staggered .delay, and the row tint lerps warmer with the score using Color interpolation. sensoryFeedback(.selection) per step. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator ramps rating 0->5->0 on a timer; stars ignite left-to-right with the staggered spring and the warmth rises automatically.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture across the row sets fractional rating (snap to whole/half); per-star spring+delay fires as each fills, sensoryFeedback(.selection) on each new star. A tap sets rating to the tapped index.

name: Stretch Pill Switch
behavior: On toggle the knob stretches into an elongated capsule mid-travel then squashes back to a circle on arrival, the track inheriting the same elastic deformation.
wow: The knob behaves like soft taffy, stretching toward its destination before snapping round again.
mechanism: An Animatable knob Capsule whose width interpolates wide at mid-travel then back to a circle, driven by .spring(bounce:0.5); offset and width share one animatableData via a custom progress value. Track inherits the squash with scaleEffect(y:). iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator toggles isOn on a timer; each flip plays the stretch->squash automatically so the tile shows the taffy elongation looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles isOn which triggers the same spring-driven stretch.

name: Swipe-Reveal Trash
behavior: Swiping a row reveals a trash action whose lid lifts and bin mouth widens as you pull further, gulping the row inward when released past threshold.
wow: The trash bin opens its mouth wider the harder you swipe and physically swallows the row on release.
mechanism: DragGesture reveals a trash action whose lid (a rotated Rectangle/Path) lifts and bin mouth widens proportionally to drag distance; past threshold the row collapses inward (height/scale->0) as if swallowed, with .spring and sensoryFeedback(.impact). iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator ramps swipe progress 0->1->0; the lid lifts, the mouth widens, the row gets gulped and resets, looping.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture translation.width drives reveal+lid open; release past threshold animates the row collapse (delete), else springs closed.

name: Tactile Like Counter Flip
behavior: A reaction count where the new digit slides up on a hinged flap from behind the old one with a split-flap snap and a tiny dust puff.
wow: The count updates via a single hinged split-flap card snap, distinct from a smooth rolling ticker.
mechanism: A hinged split-flap: top half rotation3DEffect(axis:.x) hinges down revealing the new digit behind, with a tiny Canvas dust-puff (fading particles) and a mechanical .spring settle. Distinct from a smooth numericText roll. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator increments the count on a timer; each tick plays the single hinged flap snap with the dust puff, looping.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop.

name: Thumb-Flip Like
behavior: A thumbs-up icon does a quick 3D flip on tap, rotating from outline to filled with a knuckle-crack overshoot and a faint upward float.
wow: The like flips in 3D from hollow to solid with a snappy overshoot, avoiding the overused heart entirely.
mechanism: A thumbs-up SF Symbol flips on tap via rotation3DEffect(axis:.y) with contentTransition(.symbolEffect(.replace)) swapping outline->filled at the 90 degree midpoint, plus a knuckle-crack overshoot spring and a brief upward offset float. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator toggles liked on a timer; the thumb 3D-flips outline<->filled with the overshoot and float on each cycle.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, plus a tap toggles liked driving the same 3D flip and symbol replace.

name: Trace Checkmark
behavior: A circle stroke draws clockwise via trim, then the checkmark path strokes on with a slight overshoot bounce and a single radial spark on completion.
wow: The classic success tick gains a satisfying stroke-draw with an overshoot snap and one crisp spark at the tip.
mechanism: Circle and a check Path each animated with .trim(from:0,to:progress); the check eases in after the circle, with a small spring overshoot on its end cap and one Canvas/scaleEffect radial spark on completion. sensoryFeedback(.success). iOS 17.
reference: Apple Pay / Fitness success checkmark draw
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator (or a looping withAnimation on appear) cycles progress 0->1 then resets, replaying the circle-then-check stroke with the spark on each loop.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop.

name: Fluid Volume Fill
behavior: Dragging a vertical volume control raises a liquid level that tilts and sloshes with the drag velocity before settling flat.
wow: The volume reads as liquid in a glass that sloshes and tilts as you drag, then levels out.
mechanism: A clipped container masks a liquid whose surface is an Animatable sine Path; DragGesture sets the level and feeds drag velocity into a tilt/slosh amplitude that decays via .spring. Canvas or Path+mask for the waterline. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView ramps the level up/down and injects periodic slosh so the liquid tilts, waves, and settles automatically in the tile.
interactiveSpec: Vertical DragGesture sets the fill level; drag velocity drives the slosh/tilt amplitude of the surface Path; release lets it settle level with a damped spring.
risk: slosh timing/wave amplitude is fiddly — verify the surface settles cleanly on device

name: Accordion Stretch
behavior: Pinch the button to expand it into a bellows of accordion pleats that spread apart revealing nested options, collapsing back when released.
wow: It physically concertinas open like a bellows, each fold catching light as the gap widens.
mechanism: MagnifyGesture scale drives an expansion progress; pleats are a VStack of trapezoid Shapes whose fold angle (rotation3DEffect) and gap interpolate with progress, each with a Gradient that shifts to fake light catching the fold. spring settle on release. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator drives expansion 0→1→0 auto-concertinaing the pleats open and closed, with the per-fold gradient glinting on loop.
interactiveSpec: MagnifyGesture maps magnification to expansion progress (clamped 0–1); pleat fold angles/gaps interpolate live; .onEnded springs to fully open or collapsed with sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: perf: per-pleat rotation3DEffect + gradient redraws during a continuous pinch can stutter; cap pleat count (~8) and verify on device

name: Blinds Reveal
behavior: A press rotates horizontal slats like venetian blinds, flipping from the idle label to a success state slat-by-slat with staggered timing.
wow: The label twists open one louvre at a time, a crisp mechanical sweep across the face.
mechanism: A VStack of thin slat rectangles, each rotation3DEffect about its horizontal axis with perspective; a staggered .animation delay per slat index flips them from the idle face to the success face. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView toggles the reveal every ~1.5s, auto-sweeping the slats open to the success label and closing back on loop.
interactiveSpec: Tap toggles revealed; .animation(.easeInOut) with delay = index*0.04 staggers each slat's rotation3DEffect from 0→180°; sensoryFeedback(.selection).

name: Breath Idle
behavior: While idle the button gently inhales and exhales with a soft scale and glow rhythm; on tap it holds its breath, then exhales into the success state.
wow: The button feels alive and resting, breathing calmly until you wake it with a touch.
mechanism: A manual easeInOut scale + opacity/glow (shadow radius) animation with .repeatForever(autoreverses:true) provides the breath (not symbolEffect(.breathe), which is iOS 18); tap pauses the loop, then a spring exhale to the success state. iOS 17.
previewLoop: The repeatForever easeInOut breath loop is itself the self-driving demo—scale ~0.97→1.03 with a synced glow pulse, running continuously on the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop; a tap interrupts the breath loop (hold), then a spring transition reveals the success state before resuming.

name: Conveyor Load
behavior: During loading a tiny conveyor belt scrolls labeled crates across the button, dropping each into a bin until the count completes and the belt stops.
wow: Progress is shown as a literal assembly line ferrying parcels across the button face.
mechanism: A TimelineView scrolls an HStack of crate shapes via a moving offset (belt loop); each crate reaching the bin gets a drop offset+scale animation, incrementing a counter that drives the progress and final stop. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView runs the belt continuously, auto-ferrying crates across and dropping them into the bin, resetting the count on loop.
interactiveSpec: Tap starts the TimelineView belt for N crates; the offset loops the belt texture and per-crate drop animations fire on schedule; sensoryFeedback(.success) when the count completes and the belt halts.

name: Cube Roll
behavior: Tapping rolls the button as a 3D cube tumbling one face forward, each face a different state (idle, loading, success) landing with a heavy bounce.
wow: States live on the faces of a die that physically tumbles to show the next one.
mechanism: Two visible faces are composed with rotation3DEffect about the X-axis with perspective (projectionTransform m34) and offset so the cube appears to tumble 90° per tap; a spring(bounce) lands the new face heavily. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView auto-taps every ~1.4s, rolling the cube through idle→loading→success faces with a bounce landing on loop.
interactiveSpec: Tap advances faceIndex; .animation(.spring(bounce:0.3)) rotates the cube 90° about X via rotation3DEffect, swapping the back face content at the 90° midpoint; sensoryFeedback(.impact) on landing.

name: Domino Segment
behavior: A segmented control's selection knocks each segment over like a falling domino chain that cascades to the chosen option and stands the rest back up.
wow: Switching tabs sets off a satisfying chain reaction of toppling tiles instead of a plain slide.
mechanism: Each segment is a tile with rotation3DEffect about its leading edge; a staggered spring (per-index animation delay) propagates the topple from the old selection to the new, then stands them up. matchedGeometryEffect carries the active pill. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView auto-advances the selected index every ~1.2s; each switch fires the staggered topple cascade and stand-up so the dominoes endlessly chain across the tile.
interactiveSpec: Tap sets selectedIndex; an .animation(.spring) with per-segment delay = abs(index-old)*0.05 cascades the rotation3DEffect topple, sensoryFeedback(.selection) on the chosen tile.

name: Elastic Sling
behavior: Drag the button backward like a slingshot; a rubber band stretches between two anchors and on release the button flings forward, snaps taut, and triggers the action.
wow: You load and fire the button like ammunition, with the band's tension visibly straining before launch.
mechanism: DragGesture offset moves the button against two fixed anchors; a Path draws the V-shaped band whose stroke thins (lineWidth) and tints (color interpolate) with stretch. Release fires interpolatingSpring(stiffness:280,damping:9) for the twang. iOS 17. (echoes PullToTrigger/Slingshot seed)
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator pulls the button back to a load point then snaps it forward 0→1→0, redrawing the band thinning/recoiling so the slingshot auto-fires and twangs on loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture translation sets the pull-back offset (clamped); band lineWidth/tint map to stretch magnitude; .onEnded animates the fling with interpolatingSpring + sensoryFeedback(.impact) at launch.

name: Ferrofluid Spike
behavior: On hold the button surface erupts into clustered black spikes pulled toward the touch point, collapsing back into a smooth puddle on release.
wow: Living spikes rise and chase your finger like ferrofluid reacting to a magnet under glass.
mechanism: Canvas renders a blob field with .blur + alphaThreshold (metaball technique) so merging circles form a smooth surface; spike height per node is driven toward the touch point by a LongPressGesture-tracked location, decaying via spring on release. Pure SwiftUI Canvas, no shader. iOS 17. (echoes GooeyDragBlob/Liquid Metaball Spinner seeds)
previewLoop: A TimelineView orbits a phantom magnet point around the button while the metaball Canvas raises/leans spikes toward it, auto-animating the ferrofluid crawl on loop.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture.sequenced(before: DragGesture) feeds the live touch location to the Canvas; spike amplitude rises while pressed and springs flat on release with sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: metaball needs Canvas .blur + alphaThreshold tuning and can be GPU-heavy; cap node count (~12) and verify the smooth-surface threshold on device

name: Flip-Clock Toggle
behavior: A toggle flips its state like a split-flap airport display, the top half cascading down to reveal ON or OFF with a mechanical settle.
wow: State changes land with the nostalgic flutter of a departures board flipping over.
mechanism: Two stacked halves with rotation3DEffect about the horizontal center axis (perspective in projectionTransform); the top flap rotates -90→back while the new value's bottom half is revealed mid-flip. spring(bounce) settle + sensoryFeedback(.selection). iOS 17.
