previewLoop: A TimelineView toggles state every ~1.5s, auto-firing the flap cascade ON↔OFF so the split-flap endlessly flutters on the tile.
interactiveSpec: Tap toggles isOn; .onChange drives the flap rotation3DEffect with a two-stage animation (drop then settle) and sensoryFeedback(.selection).

name: Fuse Spark
behavior: A long-press lights a fuse that burns along the button's edge with a traveling spark and trailing smoke until it reaches the end and detonates the action.
wow: Holding lights a sputtering fuse you watch race the perimeter toward an explosive confirm.
mechanism: The border Shape uses .trim(from:0,to:progress) for the burnt portion; the spark is a glowing point at the trim end (with a small Canvas/TimelineView particle sputter + smoke), progress advanced while LongPressGesture is held. Detonation = scale-pop burst + sensoryFeedback(.success). iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView auto-burns the fuse trim 0→1 with the spark racing the perimeter and sputtering, detonating and resetting on loop.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture(minimumDuration:2) advances fuse progress while pressed; spark position = path point at the trim end; releasing early lets it fizzle back, reaching 1 fires the detonation burst.
risk: perf: keep the spark/smoke Canvas particle count low (~30); verify the spark stays glued to the trim end as it races corners on device

name: Gel Jiggle
behavior: A press deforms the button like a gummy block, squashing where touched and rippling jiggle waves outward through a soft-body surface before resettling.
wow: The whole surface wobbles like jelly, deforming locally under your finger rather than uniformly scaling.
mechanism: An Animatable RoundedRectangle-like Shape whose corner control points are perturbed by a damped sine driven from the touch point; a KeyframeAnimator/spring decays the wobble. Local squash via a scale anchored at the touch location. Pure SwiftUI Animatable Shape, no shader. iOS 17. (echoes SquishyButton seed)
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator pokes a phantom touch point, firing the squash+ripple decay 0→1→0 so the gel auto-wobbles and resettles on loop.
interactiveSpec: SpatialTapGesture provides the touch location; .onEnded seeds the Animatable Shape's wobble amplitude/origin and springs it to rest with interpolatingSpring(stiffness:170,damping:6) + sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: soft-body mesh without layerEffect (shader) means hand-built Animatable Shape control points; localized squash + ripple is fiddly—verify it doesn't read as a plain scale on device

name: Ink Bleed Toggle
behavior: Toggling drops ink that bleeds outward through irregular feathered edges to flood the button in the new color, like dye spreading on wet paper.
wow: The color change spreads with organic capillary feathering instead of a clean wipe.
mechanism: A growing Circle/blob fill from the touch point as the new color, with feathered edges approximated by a blurred irregular Shape mask (or Canvas blob with .blur) animated radius 0→full; old color underneath. Pure SwiftUI mask, no colorEffect shader. iOS 17. (echoes Ink-Fill Radio seed)
previewLoop: A TimelineView toggles every ~1.5s, auto-blooming the ink from a point to flood the button in the alternate color, then bleeding back on loop.
interactiveSpec: Tap captures the touch point as the bleed origin; .animation(.easeOut(duration:0.6)) grows the blurred-edge blob mask radius from 0 to cover; sensoryFeedback(.selection).
risk: feathered edge without a shader relies on .blur on an irregular Shape; verify the capillary feathering doesn't read as a plain soft circle on device

name: Knob Detent
behavior: Rotate the circular button like a hardware dial; it clicks through notched detents with a tactile micro-rotation kickback at each step until it locks on a value.
wow: It snaps between detents with the resistance and click of a real audio gear knob.
mechanism: A DragGesture with atan2(location) computes the angle (RotationGesture also works) feeding rotationEffect; nearest-detent attraction snaps the angle, with a small overshoot spring kickback per notch and sensoryFeedback(.selection) on each detent crossing. iOS 17. (echoes RotaryKnob seed)
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator sweeps the knob angle across several detents 0→max→0, auto-clicking through notches with the micro-kickback at each on loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture(minimumDistance:0) computes angle via atan2 relative to center; rotationEffect follows; .onChanged detects detent crossings to fire sensoryFeedback(.selection); .onEnded snaps to nearest detent with an overshoot spring.

name: Magnetic Snap
behavior: As your finger nears the button, the label and a small metal nub lean toward the touch point, then snap-lock with a recoil wobble when pressed.
wow: The control feels physically magnetized to your thumb, tilting to meet you before you even touch it.
mechanism: DragGesture supplies the live touch point; an attraction curve maps finger distance to a lean offset/rotationEffect on the nub, and release fires a spring(response:0.3,dampingFraction:0.45) recoil plus sensoryFeedback(.impact). iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator cycles a phantom touch point in from the edge to center and back (0→1→0), driving the same lean+snap so the nub auto-leans, snaps, and wobbles on loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture(minimumDistance:0) tracks location.x/y; distance from button center feeds an inverse-falloff attraction into the nub offset and rotationEffect; .onEnded springs back with sensoryFeedback(.impact, trigger:).

name: Mercury Fill
behavior: On press a blob of liquid metal drops in and sloshes side to side under gravity, settling to fill the button as a progress level with a reflective meniscus.
wow: The fill has real inertia, overshooting and rebalancing like mercury in a tilted vial.
mechanism: Canvas draws the fill level with an animatable sine-wave surface path; a damped spring on the tilt/amplitude value produces the slosh, and a LinearGradient meniscus highlight rides the surface. Pure SwiftUI Canvas, no shader. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A KeyframeAnimator runs fill 0→1 with independent slosh-amplitude and tilt tracks, auto-pouring and settling the mercury on loop; meniscus glint cross-fades at the top.
interactiveSpec: Tap toggles a fill target 0↔1; .onChange animates with interpolatingSpring(stiffness:120,damping:8) so the level overshoots and rebalances, sensoryFeedback(.impact) on settle.
risk: metaball-like surface needs Canvas path + spring tuning; verify slosh decay reads as metal, not water, on device

name: Paper Plane Fold
behavior: Tapping folds the rectangular button along diagonal creases into a paper airplane that launches off-screen, then unfolds back on reset.
wow: A flat button becomes 3D paper that creases, folds, and flies, all with pure SwiftUI rotation3D.
mechanism: Triangular Shape panels fold via rotation3DEffect about diagonal axes with perspective in the projectionTransform; a PhaseAnimator sequences crease folds then a launch offset+rotation. Per-panel gradient gives crease shading. iOS 17.
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator cycles phases idle→creasing→folded→launch(offset/scale out)→reset, auto-folding the plane and gliding it off the tile, then unfolding on loop.
interactiveSpec: Tap advances a PhaseAnimator trigger through fold→launch; rotation3DEffect axes are fixed to the diagonal creases, with sensoryFeedback(.impact) at the launch frame.

name: Piston Pump
behavior: A hold-to-confirm shows a mechanical piston pumping up and down, each stroke filling a pressure gauge until it redlines and fires.
wow: Confirming feels like building pressure in an engine, with the piston visibly cycling faster as you hold.
mechanism: A repeating KeyframeAnimator/PhaseAnimator drives the piston offset (up/down strokes); stroke period shortens as a held-progress value rises, feeding a trimmed AngularGradient gauge arc. Redline at to:1 triggers a spring pop + sensoryFeedback(.success). iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView auto-holds: the piston pumps with accelerating cadence while the gauge fills 0→1, redlines and fires, then resets on loop.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture(minimumDuration:2.5) increments held-progress while pressed, which shortens the piston KeyframeAnimator period and advances the gauge trim; release before redline drains it back.
risk: perf: keep the piston KeyframeAnimator and gauge on one timeline so cadence stays synced; verify the speed-up reads smoothly on device

name: Sand Pour
behavior: On tap the button drains its grains downward like an hourglass, particles streaming through a pinch to refill a lower chamber that morphs into the success label.
wow: Thousands of falling grains flow through a pinch and pile up convincingly using a single Canvas.
mechanism: Canvas + TimelineView integrate per-grain position/velocity/gravity through a narrow neck, accumulating into a settling pile; the lower fill height drives a contentTransition/opacity crossfade into the success label. Pure SwiftUI Canvas, no shader. iOS 17. (echoes Velocity Confetti / Starfield Canvas seeds)
previewLoop: A TimelineView auto-pours grains 0→1 every cycle, draining the top chamber into the pile and morphing to the success label, then resetting on loop.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop, triggered by tap: tapping starts the TimelineView particle integrator for one full pour, sensoryFeedback(.success) when the pile completes.
risk: perf: cap grain count (~300–500) and reuse a fixed particle buffer; verify frame rate of the Canvas integrator on device

name: Shatter Reform
behavior: An error state shatters the button into angular glass shards that scatter and then magnetically reassemble back into the intact button.
wow: The button breaks apart into believable shards and then plays the break in reverse to heal itself.
mechanism: A set of precomputed triangular shard Shapes (Voronoi-ish) each get a per-shard offset/rotation/opacity driven by a KeyframeAnimator; scatter phase explodes them outward, reform phase springs them back to their home transform. Pure SwiftUI Shapes, no shader. iOS 17. (echoes Glass Shatter seed)
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator cycles intact→shatter(scatter)→reform, auto-exploding and reassembling the shards on loop.
interactiveSpec: Tap triggers the PhaseAnimator through scatter→reform; each shard's offset/rotation interpolates from its home transform; sensoryFeedback(.error) at the shatter frame, (.success) on reform.
risk: perf: precompute shard geometry once (don't regenerate per frame); keep shard count modest (~20) and verify reassembly snap on device

name: Static Discharge
behavior: Drag near the button to build a crackling electric arc that jumps the gap to your finger, and the completed circuit discharges to trigger the press.
wow: A jagged lightning arc leaps to meet your finger, closing the circuit with a satisfying zap.
mechanism: DragGesture tracks the finger; a jittered jagged Path (random midpoint-displacement bolt, regenerated on a TimelineView tick) draws between the button and touch point, opacity/brightness rising as the gap closes; discharge = flash + sensoryFeedback(.impact). Pure SwiftUI Path/Canvas, no shader. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView animates a phantom finger approaching, with the jittering bolt Path crackling brighter as the gap narrows, discharging in a flash and resetting on loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture(minimumDistance:0) location feeds the bolt's endpoint; a TimelineView re-jitters the bolt path each tick; when finger distance < threshold the circuit discharges with a flash and sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: perf: regenerating the jagged bolt path each TimelineView tick is cheap but verify it doesn't strobe harshly; tune jitter and flash duration on device

name: Stitch Thread
behavior: Tapping animates a needle drawing thread stitch-by-stitch around the button's border, sewing a dashed seam that completes into a confirmed outline.
wow: Each loading step is literally sewn on, with a needle and trailing thread tracing the perimeter.
mechanism: A RoundedRectangle border Shape with .stroke(style: dash) animated via .trim(from:0,to:progress); a needle image rides the path end using the trailing trim point, progress animated with a linear/easeInOut over the loading duration. sensoryFeedback(.success) on completion. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView/PhaseAnimator runs trim 0→1→0 auto-sewing the dashed seam around the border with the needle riding the leading edge, then unstitching on loop.
interactiveSpec: Tap animates trim progress 0→1 with .animation(.easeInOut(duration:1.2)); the needle's position is derived from the path point at the current trim end; sensoryFeedback(.success) at to:1.

name: Tear Perforation
behavior: Swipe along a dotted perforation line to rip the button like a coupon stub; the torn edge frays with jagged paper detail and reveals the action underneath.
wow: The ragged tearing edge follows your finger exactly, like ripping a real ticket in half.
mechanism: An Animatable jagged Shape (random-seeded zig-zag whose tear-progress is animatableData) masks the two halves; DragGesture.x drives progress, and past threshold a spring detaches the stub which falls with offset+rotation. iOS 17. (echoes TearAwayCoupon seed)
previewLoop: A TimelineView/KeyframeAnimator drives tear progress 0→1 then resets, auto-ripping the stub along the ragged path and fluttering it away before re-forming on loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture translation.width maps to tear progress along the perforation; the jagged Shape's animatableData = progress; .onEnded past 0.6 springs the stub free with sensoryFeedback(.impact).
risk: ragged edge needs a stable per-instance random seed so the tear line doesn't jitter between frames; verify on device

name: Thermal Press
behavior: Holding the button heats it through color stages from cool blue to glowing orange with a rising heat-shimmer haze, releasing lets it cool and fade.
wow: The longer you hold the more it visibly glows and radiates, like pressing a branding iron.
mechanism: LongPressGesture drives a 0→1 heat value mapped through a Gradient (blue→orange) fill plus a growing shadow/glow radius; shimmer approximated by a blurred wavy overlay shape oscillated via TimelineView (no distortionEffect, shaders disallowed for Buttons). iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator ramps heat 0→1→0 auto-heating the button through the color stages with the shimmer overlay oscillating, then cooling on loop.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture(minimumDuration:2) with a parallel timer raises the heat value while pressed; release animates it back to 0 with easeOut, sensoryFeedback(.warning) at redline.
risk: true heat-shimmer would want distortionEffect (a shader, disallowed here); the blurred wavy-overlay approximation can read flat—verify it sells the shimmer on device

name: Vault Dial
behavior: Rotate the button left and right like a safe combination dial; correct stops illuminate, and the final turn swings the button open like a vault door.
wow: Unlocking feels like cracking a safe, ending with a heavy 3D door swing revealing the content.
mechanism: DragGesture atan2 angle (or RotationGesture) drives the dial rotationEffect; a sequence-matcher lights correct stops with sensoryFeedback(.selection) ticks, and the final match triggers a rotation3DEffect Y-axis door swing (perspective) revealing content. iOS 17. (echoes DialPadKnob/RotaryKnob seeds)
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator auto-spins the dial through the correct sequence (CW→CCW→CW), lighting each stop, then swings the vault door open and resets on loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture computes angle via atan2; .onChanged tracks rotation direction reversals and matches stops; final stop triggers the rotation3DEffect door swing with sensoryFeedback(.success).
risk: perf/logic: direction-reversal detection plus the 3D door swing are stateful; verify the swing perspective and that mid-turn reversals don't desync the matcher on device

name: Zipper Confirm
behavior: A long-press drags a zipper pull across the button, interlocking teeth meshing behind it until it fully closes and locks into a confirmed state.
wow: Confirming feels like sealing a bag shut, with each tooth meshing in sequence under your fingertip.
mechanism: A sequenced LongPressGesture.sequenced(before: DragGesture) drives a 0→1 close progress; teeth are an HStack of small triangle Shapes whose interlock is keyed to progress, and a ZStack mask reveals the meshed half. spring settle + sensoryFeedback(.success) on lock. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView/PhaseAnimator drives close progress 0→1 then snaps open, auto-running the pull head left-to-right so teeth mesh and the lock latches on loop.
interactiveSpec: LongPressGesture(minimumDuration:0.1).sequenced(before: DragGesture()) maps drag.x to close progress, clamps 0–1; past 0.95 it auto-completes with a spring and sensoryFeedback(.success).
risk: perf: keep tooth count low (~16) so per-tooth Shape redraws stay cheap; verify mesh seam alignment on device

name: Clock Pie Wipe
behavior: An angular sweep mask drawn in Canvas rotates around the center like a clock hand, progressively unveiling the next view in a radial pie sweep.
wow: A bright leading-edge highlight rides the sweeping radius like a radar beam, making the angular wipe feel mechanical and precise.
mechanism: An Animatable pie/wedge Shape (addArc with a sweep endAngle bound to progress) masks the destination view; a thin bright LinearGradient ray sits at the leading radius. Driven by withAnimation/TimelineView. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView or PhaseAnimator loops the sweep angle 0→360° continuously, the leading-edge highlight rotating like a radar beam revealing the next view each cycle inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Column Slat Slide
behavior: The outgoing view is split into vertical column slats that slide off-screen at staggered speeds and offsets, the next view sliding in behind each gap.
wow: The staggered timing creates a rippling vertical-blind cascade where columns appear to overtake each other in a satisfying mechanical wave.
mechanism: The outgoing view is sliced into N vertical strips (each a clipped copy via .mask of a column rect); a progress value offsets each strip with a per-index delay/speed, the incoming view sitting behind. Driven by withAnimation/TimelineView. iOS 17.
reference: Vertical-blind slide wipe, slat curtain transition
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the slide progress 0→1→0 so the vertical slats cascade off and back with staggered timing continuously, the rippling wave replaying inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Crumple Fade
behavior: The outgoing view scales inward while a crinkle-gradient overlay creases it into a crumpled-paper look, fading out as the next view eases in beneath.
wow: Faceted light-and-shadow creases bloom across the surface as it shrinks, faking a paper crumple convincingly with layered gradient overlays.
mechanism: The outgoing view scaleEffect shrinks while layered angular LinearGradient overlays (faceted light/shadow creases) ramp in opacity and the view fades out; driven by withAnimation(.easeIn). Pure overlays, no shader. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops a crumple progress 0→1→0 so the view shrinks with crease overlays blooming and fades, then un-crumples back, replaying continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles a crumpled Bool driving withAnimation(.spring/.easeIn) the crumple progress; the scale, crease-overlay opacity, and fade all interpolate from that single value.

name: Cube Tumble
behavior: Pages map to the faces of a 3D cube that rotates through 90-degree turns with perspective, advancing one view per face.
wow: You can drag to scrub the cube mid-rotation and it rubber-bands to the nearest face on release; left alone it auto-tumbles through faces on a loop.
mechanism: A custom GeometryEffect sets the projection m34 for true perspective and rotates two adjacent faces about a shared edge; rotation angle is driven by a continuous progress value, snapping to the nearest 90° face with a spring. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator auto-cycles the cube rotation through successive 90° faces on a loop, the perspective foreshortening playing as each face tumbles into view inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: Horizontal DragGesture translation maps to the cube rotation angle (scrubbable mid-turn); on release it springs/rubber-bands to the nearest 90° face, using predictedEndTranslation to decide whether to advance or snap back.

name: Depth Stack Push
behavior: The current card recedes into a z-ordered stack with scale-down and progressive blur while the incoming card rises forward into focus, like a physical deck being shuffled in 3D space.
wow: Background cards stay subtly visible and parallax as you swipe, giving a true sense of layered depth instead of a flat slide. Auto-demo cycles the stack on a loop.
mechanism: A ZStack of cards offset by index; a swipe progress drives scaleEffect, .blur(radius:), and yOffset per index so cards recede or advance, with a spring settle on commit. Pure transforms, no shader. iOS 17.
reference: DepthScrollCards / StackPushPeek — App Store Today cards
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator auto-advances the stack one card per phase on a loop — front card recedes and blurs into the deck while the next rises forward — so the depth shuffle plays itself in the tile.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture horizontal translation maps to a 0→1 push progress driving the per-index scale/blur/offset; release past a threshold (or predictedEndTranslation) commits the push with interactiveSpring, otherwise springs back.

name: Drawer Cascade
behavior: Stacked horizontal drawer panels slide open sequentially from a closed cabinet, each sliding out further than the last with depth shadows, the open drawers revealing the destination content.
wow: Sequential staggered slide timing plus deepening drop-shadows give a real sense of physical drawers being pulled out one after another.
mechanism: N drawer panels in a ZStack each offset horizontally by a per-index amount; an open progress with staggered per-index delays slides each out further than the last, with .shadow depth scaling by index. Driven by withAnimation(.spring). iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the open progress 0→1→0 so the drawers slide out in sequence then retract continuously, the deepening shadows replaying each cycle inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles an isOpen Bool driving withAnimation(.spring) the open progress; each drawer's horizontal offset and shadow depth interpolate from it with staggered per-index delays.

name: Fan Deck
behavior: A stack of cards fans out radially like a held hand of playing cards, then collapses back into a single stack centered on the chosen card.
wow: The cards spread with staggered rotation around a pivot and recollapse with a satisfying spring snap, exactly like fanning real cards.
mechanism: Each card gets a rotationEffect about a shared bottom pivot anchor (.bottom) plus a radial offset; a fan progress maps per-index to a spread angle, animated with .spring(bounce:) for the snap. Pure transforms. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the fan progress 0→1→0 so the deck fans out radially then snaps back into a stack continuously, the staggered card rotations replaying inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles a fanned Bool driving withAnimation(.spring(bounce:0.3)) the fan progress; per-card spread angles interpolate from it, and tapping a fanned card recollapses the stack centered on that index.

name: Flip Book
behavior: Multiple pages rapidly flip past in book style with motion blur on the turning leaf, landing on the target page; a drag scrubs the flip-through.
wow: Drag to riffle through pages at any speed with blur smearing the fast turns, or release to let it spring to a page; auto-demo riffles on a loop.
mechanism: Pages stacked with the top leaf using rotation3DEffect about the spine (.y axis, leading anchor) for the turn; a continuous page-progress drives which leaf turns, and .blur(radius:) scales with turn speed for the riffle smear. Pure 3D transforms. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator/TimelineView loops the page-progress riffling forward then back so leaves flip past with motion blur on the fast turns, replaying the riffle continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: Horizontal/vertical DragGesture maps translation+velocity to page-progress so the user riffles at any speed (blur scales with drag velocity); release springs to the nearest whole page, using predictedEndTranslation to decide how many leaves to coast through.

name: Fold-Down Accordion
behavior: Stacked list rows concertina-collapse along shared horizontal hinges, each row folding flat against the next with hinge shadows, compressing into a single header.
wow: Adjacent rows share a darkening hinge crease so the collapse reads as one continuous folding ribbon rather than independent rows shrinking.
mechanism: Each row uses rotation3DEffect about its top edge (.x axis) with a perspective anchor; a collapse progress folds rows flat in sequence (staggered ranges) and a shared LinearGradient hinge overlay darkens at each crease. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops collapse progress 0→1→0 so the stacked rows concertina shut into the header then unfold open again continuously, hinge shadows deepening each cycle inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture on the header toggles a collapsed Bool driving withAnimation(.spring) the collapse progress; the per-row hinge angles interpolate from it with staggered offsets.

name: Genie Warp
behavior: The outgoing view squeezes and stretches into a narrowing tapered neck that sucks down toward a target point, like the macOS minimize-to-Dock genie, using an animatable warp Shape.
wow: The content visibly bends and tapers along the warp curve as it's pulled into the point, a signature 'whoa' effect rarely seen on iOS in pure SwiftUI.
mechanism: An Animatable warp Shape (or GeometryEffect) deforms the view's bounding path into a tapering S-neck via animatableData (0→1), used as a clip/mask while the view scales/offsets toward the target point; warp control points interpolate over progress. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the genie progress 0→1→0 so the view sucks down into the target point as a tapering neck then springs back out, the warp replaying continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles a minimized Bool driving withAnimation(.spring) the warp progress; the Animatable warp Shape's animatableData interpolates the taper while the view scales/offsets toward the dock point, reversing to restore.
risk: animatable warp Shape with custom animatableData; verify the neck interpolation stays smooth and doesn't kink mid-warp on device

name: Glass Shatter
behavior: The outgoing view fractures into dozens of Canvas-drawn triangular glass shards that tumble, spin, and fall away under gravity, uncovering the view behind.
wow: Shards catch a raking specular highlight as they rotate, so the break sparkles like real broken glass before clearing the frame.
mechanism: A precomputed Voronoi/triangular fracture set is rendered in a Canvas, each shard given per-shard velocity, gravity, and spin integrated against a TimelineView clock; a LinearGradient specular per shard rotates with its angle for the glint. iOS 17.
reference: Shatter Reform button break, glass-break screen effects
previewLoop: A TimelineView loops the shatter: shards explode out under gravity, then the timeline resets so they re-assemble and shatter again, giving a self-playing break inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture sets a shattered Bool that starts the TimelineView-driven fall; the shard physics run from tap until offscreen, then a reverse phase reassembles on the next tap.
risk: perf: cap shard count (~30-40) and reuse a precomputed fracture mesh; verify Canvas redraw cost on device

name: Hero Zoom Scatter
behavior: A tapped grid thumbnail expands to a full detail view via matchedGeometryEffect while every surrounding grid cell scatters outward and fades, then settles back on dismiss.
wow: The siblings physically flee the hero with staggered springs, so the focused item feels like it's pushing the whole grid aside as it grows.
mechanism: matchedGeometryEffect shares the hero frame between the grid cell and the full detail view (the iOS 17 path, no zoom navigation transition); sibling cells get an outward offset keyed to their direction from the hero center plus opacity, animated with staggered .spring delays. iOS 17.
reference: Zoom Card to Detail — Photos / App Store hero zoom
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator auto-cycles select→dismiss on a loop: one tile grows via matchedGeometryEffect while siblings scatter and fade, then everything settles back, replaying continuously in the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture sets a selected id driving withAnimation(.spring) the matchedGeometryEffect expansion; siblings read the selection to compute their outward scatter offset/opacity with staggered spring delays, reversing on dismiss tap.

name: Iris Shutter Wipe
behavior: A ring of overlapping camera-aperture blades drawn in Canvas spirals open from the center, the polygonal opening growing to reveal the destination view.
wow: The blades rotate as they open so the aperture's polygon edges sweep like a real lens iris, not a plain circle expanding.
mechanism: A Canvas draws N curved aperture blades each rotated about an off-center pivot; an open progress both rotates the blades and grows the central polygonal opening, which is used as a mask (Canvas as mask / drawLayer) over the destination view. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the iris open progress 0→1→0 so the blades spiral open to reveal the next view then spiral shut again continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles an isOpen Bool driving withAnimation(.spring) the iris progress; the blade rotation and polygon-opening radius interpolate from that single value to wipe the destination in or out.

name: Liquid Drip Reveal
behavior: The top view's bottom edge develops Canvas-drawn droplets that elongate and drip downward, the surface melting away to expose the view beneath.
wow: Individual droplets bulge, neck, and detach with surface-tension timing, so the layer dissolves like melting wax rather than wiping.
mechanism: A Canvas (with blur + alphaThreshold for surface-tension blobbing) draws the top view's lower edge as a row of droplets whose lengths grow on a TimelineView clock; the Canvas output masks the top view so it melts away as droplets detach, revealing the view beneath. iOS 17.
reference: Melt-In / liquid drip dissolve, wax-melt reveal
previewLoop: A TimelineView loops the drip: droplets elongate, neck, detach, and the surface melts away to reveal the next view, then resets so the melt replays continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: metaball drip needs Canvas blur+alphaThreshold for the necking; cap droplet count and verify the surface-tension threshold on device

name: Magnetic Swap
behavior: Two cards swap positions when dragged, snapping together with a springy magnetic pull and a slight overshoot recoil as they lock into each other's slots.
wow: As the dragged card nears the swap threshold the other card leans toward it as if magnetized, then both snap with a tactile recoil. Auto-demo swaps them on a loop.
mechanism: Two cards positioned by slot; a drag offset on one drives a 0→1 swap progress, and as it crosses a threshold the other card eases toward its new slot via a magnetic attraction curve, both settling with .spring(bounce:) overshoot plus sensoryFeedback on snap. iOS 17.
reference: MagneticDragGrid / Magnetic Snap Grid — Home-screen slot snap
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator auto-cycles the swap progress 0→1→0 on a loop so the two cards trade slots with the magnetic lean and overshoot recoil, replaying continuously in the tile.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture on a card maps translation to the swap progress; once past the midpoint threshold the other card animates toward the vacated slot via the attraction curve, and release commits both with .spring(bounce:) and a sensoryFeedback(.impact) tick (snap-back if under threshold).

name: Metaball Merge Reveal
behavior: Two rounded pill blobs slide toward each other inside a Canvas with blur + alphaThreshold filters, fusing with a gooey liquid pinch that resolves into the next view's container.
wow: The crisp organic neck-pinch between the blobs is real metaball physics done with pure SwiftUI Canvas filters, not a soft cross-fade.
mechanism: A Canvas draws two filled capsules into a layer with .blur then .alphaThreshold applied via Canvas symbols/drawLayer filters, so overlapping blurred edges snap into a gooey neck; a TimelineView advances the blob centers toward each other and the resolved container clips/masks the destination view. iOS 17.
reference: Liquid Blob Merge — Dynamic Island gooey morph
previewLoop: A TimelineView drives the two blob centers together and apart on a loop so the gooey neck forms, pinches, and separates repeatedly inside the tile with no input.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: metaball needs Canvas blur+alphaThreshold; verify the neck-pinch threshold tuning on device and keep blob count to 2

name: Mosaic Tile Flip
behavior: A 2D grid of tiles flips one-by-one in a diagonal wave order, each tile rotating on rotation3DEffect to swap its face from the old view to the new one.
wow: The diagonal flip wave makes the new image assemble itself tile by tile across the screen, like a departures board reshuffling.
mechanism: Both views are sliced into a tile grid; each tile uses rotation3DEffect about .y with a back-face swap at 90°, and a flip progress staggered by (row+col) creates the diagonal wave. Pure 3D transforms. iOS 17.
reference: Card Flip Reveal / split-flap departures board
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the flip progress 0→1→0 so the tiles flip across the diagonal to assemble the new face then flip back, the wave replaying continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles a flipped Bool driving withAnimation(.spring) the flip progress; each tile's rotation3DEffect angle is offset by its (row+col) diagonal index so the wave reads, swapping faces at 90°.
risk: perf: a fine grid means many rotation3DEffect layers — keep tile count modest (e.g. 6x8) and verify flip wave smoothness on device

name: Origami Unfold
behavior: A folded card opens along three creased panels that rotate in sequence on alternating hinge axes, revealing the destination view as the paper flattens.
wow: Each panel casts a soft crease-shadow on the one beneath it, so it reads as real folded paper unfurling rather than a flat flip.
mechanism: Three stacked panels each with rotation3DEffect on alternating .x/.y axes and a perspective-baked anchor; a single 0→1 progress (driven by withAnimation(.spring)) maps to the three hinge angles with staggered ranges, while a LinearGradient overlay per panel darkens toward each crease for the shadow. iOS 17.
reference: Paper-fold reveals (Headspace onboarding), physical greeting-card unfold
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator cycles a fold progress 0→1→0 on a loop so the three panels unfold then refold continuously inside the tile, with the crease shadows breathing as it plays.
interactiveSpec: Button/onTapGesture toggles an isOpen Bool driving withAnimation(.spring(duration:0.6)) the fold progress forward to unfold and reverse to refold; the three hinge angles interpolate from the single progress value.

name: Page Peel
behavior: A corner-anchored faked page curl lifts and rotates away via rotation3DEffect with a gradient shadow gutter and a tinted back-of-page, revealing the next page underneath.
wow: The curl shadow and back-page tint sell a convincing paper peel using only SwiftUI 3D transforms; drag the corner to control the curl, or watch it peel itself in demo.
mechanism: The top page uses rotation3DEffect about a diagonal axis anchored at the lifted corner; a curl progress drives the angle, a LinearGradient shadow gutter widens along the fold, and a tinted back-of-page view shows through. Pure 3D transforms. iOS 17.
reference: PeelAway — Calendar page curl / sticker peel
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the curl progress 0→1→0 so the corner peels up to reveal the next page then lays back down repeatedly, the shadow gutter sweeping each cycle inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture on the corner maps finger distance to the curl progress driving the rotation3DEffect angle and shadow width; release past a threshold springs the page fully off (advance), otherwise springs it flat again.

name: Spotlight Sweep
behavior: A moving radial spotlight mask travels across the screen, and wherever its beam falls the next view is revealed while the rest stays in the previous state.
wow: A soft penumbra falloff at the beam's edge makes content fade up gradually as the light passes, like a torch sweeping a dark room. Drag to aim the beam, or let it sweep automatically.
mechanism: A RadialGradient (opaque center → clear edge for the penumbra) positioned at a beam point is used as a .mask over the destination view; the beam center is driven by a progress value (or finger). Pure SwiftUI mask, no shader. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView/PhaseAnimator loops the beam center sweeping across the tile so the destination view is revealed under the moving spotlight and re-hidden behind it, replaying continuously.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture location sets the RadialGradient mask center directly so the user aims the beam; revealed regions persist (or fade back) as the finger moves, with a spring easing the beam toward the touch point.

name: Taffy Bridge Morph
behavior: A source shape stretches a thinning 1D liquid bridge toward the destination position, the connecting strand necking and snapping as the shape arrives and re-rounds.
wow: The single elastic strand thins to a hair and snaps with surface-tension recoil, a focused taffy-pull morph distinct from a full blob merge.
mechanism: A Canvas (blur + alphaThreshold) draws the source and destination blobs plus a connecting strand whose waist thins as the source travels; a TimelineView/progress moves the source and tapers the strand until it necks, snaps, and the shape re-rounds. iOS 17.
reference: Liquid Blob Merge family — taffy-pull strand morph
previewLoop: A TimelineView loops the source traveling to the destination so the taffy strand stretches, thins, necks, and snaps then resets, replaying the pull continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: metaball strand needs Canvas blur+alphaThreshold; the thin-neck snap is the fragile part — tune the threshold and verify the strand doesn't break early on device

name: Theater Curtain
behavior: Two gathered velvet curtain panels, drawn as pleated Paths, draw apart from the center with realistic fabric bunching to reveal the content behind.
wow: The pleats compress and widen as the curtain gathers, with a sheen gradient that shifts across the folds for a genuine stage-reveal moment.
mechanism: Each curtain is an Animatable Shape whose Path draws sine-based pleat waves; an openFraction (0→1, animated with withAnimation(.spring)) compresses the pleat wavelength as the panel narrows toward the edge, and a moving LinearGradient sheen rides the folds. iOS 17.
reference: Stage curtain reveal, Keynote curtain transition
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops openFraction 0→1→0 so the two pleated panels part and re-gather continuously, the sheen sweeping across the bunching folds each cycle inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles an isOpen Bool driving withAnimation(.spring) the openFraction forward to part the curtains and reverse to close; the Animatable Shape interpolates pleat compression smoothly.

name: Venetian Blinds
behavior: Horizontal slats flip in unison on their long axis via rotation3DEffect, raking light across each slat as it turns edge-on, then face-on to compose the next view.
wow: A directional light gradient sweeps along the 1D row of slats as they tilt, mimicking real venetian blinds catching window light.
mechanism: N horizontal slats each rotation3DEffect about the .x axis; a flip progress turns them edge-on (revealing the new face), and a LinearGradient brightness keyed to the tilt angle rakes light across each slat. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the flip progress 0→1→0 so the slats rotate edge-on to swap faces then flip back, the light rake sweeping across them each cycle inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles a flipped Bool driving withAnimation(.spring) the flip progress; each slat's rotation3DEffect angle and gradient brightness interpolate from that single value.

name: Vortex Spiral
behavior: The outgoing view rotates and scales down into a vanishing spiral while concentric ring overlays sweep inward, then the next view un-spirals back out to full size.
wow: The combined rotation, scale, and inward ring sweep creates a swirling drain-down illusion using only transforms and overlay rings, no shader.
mechanism: rotationEffect + scaleEffect on the outgoing view driven by a spiral progress, combined with concentric Circle stroke overlays whose radii sweep inward; the incoming view runs the same in reverse. Pure transforms + overlays, no shader. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the spiral progress 0→1→0 so the view spins and shrinks into the vortex with rings sweeping inward then un-spirals back out, replaying continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture toggles a spiraled Bool driving withAnimation(.spring/.easeInOut) the spiral progress; the rotation, scale, and concentric ring radii all interpolate from that single value, reversing to un-spiral.

name: Zipper Unzip
behavior: Two halves of the view are joined by interleaving Path-drawn zipper teeth along a center seam; a slider pulls down the seam, separating the halves to reveal the next view.
wow: The teeth mesh and unmesh tooth-by-tooth as the puller descends, an unmistakably tactile zipper you can drag open or watch unzip itself.
mechanism: Two half-views offset apart below the puller position; zipper teeth are a Path of interleaved trapezoids whose mesh/unmesh is keyed to the puller's y (teeth above stay meshed, below separate). A progress value sets puller y and the half-view spread. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator loops the puller y 0→1→0 so the seam unzips tooth-by-tooth to reveal the next view then re-zips, replaying continuously inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: Vertical DragGesture on the puller maps translation to its y position, which drives the seam separation and tooth meshing; release uses velocity to spring the zipper fully open (advance) or back closed (snap-back), with sensoryFeedback ticks per tooth.

name: Letter Confetti Drop
behavior: The word explodes into its individual characters which tumble and fall with gravity, bounce off the bottom edge, and then magnetically reassemble back into the word with a spring as the loop repeats.
wow: Flinging the word physically scatters the glyphs with real gravity and floor bounce before they gather themselves back into legible text.
mechanism: A TextRenderer exposes per-glyph slices; a TimelineView integrates each glyph's velocity/gravity/rotation and floor-bounce during the explode phase, then lerps every glyph back to its layout home with a spring for the reassemble. iOS 18 (TextRenderer).
reference: Confetti Cannon / Velocity Confetti Fling (Canvas+TimelineView particles)
previewLoop: The TimelineView auto-cycles explode (gravity + bounce) then magnetic reassemble on a ~3s loop, so the word keeps bursting and re-forming with no finger.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture velocity seeds each glyph's initial launch vector on release (fling); the same TimelineView physics integrator then runs the fall, bounce and spring-back reassembly.
risk: TextRenderer requires iOS 18; cap glyph count and step the integrator at a fixed dt to keep the bounce stable

name: Glyph Decode Cascade
behavior: Text resolves out of a column of rapidly cycling random glyphs per slot, where each character locks from left to right and the still-scrambling characters dim and jitter while the locked ones brighten and settle.
wow: The brightness handoff between locked and still-cycling characters gives a satisfying wave of order sweeping across the chaos, distinct from chromatic glitch.
mechanism: A TimelineView ticks ~20fps; each slot in an HStack of per-character Text shows a random glyph until its per-index lock time passes, then snaps to the target and animates opacity/brightness up while unlocked slots stay dim and jittered. iOS 17.
previewLoop: The TimelineView restarts the lock-time schedule every ~2s, so the left-to-right resolve-from-noise cascade plays continuously without input.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture resets all per-slot lock times to now and replays the scramble-then-resolve cascade; same TimelineView driver as the preview.

name: Per-Character Pop
behavior: Characters spring in from zero scale with an overshoot bounce one after another, each landing with a tiny squash-and-stretch and a soft shadow that settles, like letters dropping onto a trampoline surface.
wow: The squash-and-stretch on each landing gives cheap-to-ship per-letter entrances a genuinely physical, playful bounce.
mechanism: HStack of per-character Text, each entering with a staggered spring(bounce:0.5) on scaleEffect from 0, plus a brief non-uniform x/y squash keyframe on landing and an animated shadow. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator replays the staggered pop-in (and a pop-out) on a ~2s loop so letters keep springing onto the baseline inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture replays the staggered per-letter spring pop-in with the squash-and-stretch landing; same stagger as the preview.

name: Elastic Rope Text
behavior: Dragging anywhere on the line pulls the nearest characters toward your finger on damped springs, stretching the baseline like a rubber band, and releasing snaps everything back with a traveling recoil wave; idle tiles ripple in a gentle auto sine.
wow: Per-glyph spring displacement toward a live touch point turns a static title into a tactile rubber band you can pluck and watch recoil.
mechanism: A TextRenderer reads Text.Layout and offsets each glyph by a distance-falloff vector toward the drag location, with per-glyph spring(bounce:) recoil; animatableData carries the touch point and a recoil-wave phase. iOS 18 (TextRenderer).
previewLoop: When idle a TimelineView feeds a sine sweep as a virtual touch point traveling along the line, so glyphs ripple and recoil on a self-driving loop.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture location feeds the TextRenderer's touch point; nearby glyphs spring toward it while dragging and snap back with a traveling recoil wave on release.

name: Origami Letter Unfold
behavior: Each character unfolds into place from a flat folded triangle, hinging open across two creases with 3D rotation and a gradient fold-shadow that resolves to a flat face, staggered like a paper fan opening.
wow: The two-axis crease shadows during the unfold give a convincing folded-paper depth, like the title is being origami-opened from a single sheet.
mechanism: Per-character glyph split into halves via .mask, each half hinging open with rotation3DEffect across two axes and a LinearGradient crease-shadow overlay that fades as the angle approaches flat; a per-letter delay staggers the fan. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator folds the letters shut then unfolds them open on a ~2.5s loop, so the staggered origami fan keeps opening and closing.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture replays the staggered unfold from folded triangles to flat faces; same rotation3DEffect + crease-shadow sequence as the preview.

name: Gooey Letter Merge
behavior: Characters slide together and their soft blurred-threshold edges fuse into gooey metaball blobs, then crisp apart into clean letters, looping the melt-and-resolve via a blur plus contrast-threshold mask in Canvas.
wow: The metaball gooeyness where adjacent letters stretch toward and bridge each other is mesmerizing, achieved purely with blur-threshold compositing, no shaders.
mechanism: Letters drawn in a Canvas with addFilter(.blur) then addFilter(.alphaThreshold) so overlapping blurred glyphs fuse into metaball bridges; a TimelineView animates inter-letter spacing closed then open to melt and crisp. Pure Canvas, no Metal shaders. iOS 17.
reference: Liquid Metaball Spinner / Liquid Blob Merge (Canvas blur+threshold)
previewLoop: The TimelineView loops spacing-closed (merge) then spacing-open (resolve) every ~2.5s, so letters keep gooily fusing and separating on their own.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: Canvas blur + alphaThreshold metaball; verify threshold/blur radii so bridges form without smearing thin strokes, check on device

name: Liquid Gradient Flow
behavior: A flowing multi-stop gradient travels through the text mask with the colors warping along a sine path, so the hues appear to pour and swirl inside the glyphs rather than sweeping in a flat straight line.
wow: The sine-warped color flow makes the gradient look like liquid currents moving through the letters, well beyond a basic linear shimmer sweep.
mechanism: A wide multi-stop LinearGradient (optionally several offset bands) is .mask(Text); a TimelineView animates its offset and a sine-driven start/end skew so the color flow curves rather than runs straight. iOS 17.
reference: Shimmer Highlight Word (gradient-masked text), warped into a flow
previewLoop: The TimelineView continuously scrolls and sine-warps the gradient through the glyph mask, so colors keep pouring through the letters on a loop.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Handwriting Stroke
behavior: A script headline draws itself stroke by stroke as if written by an invisible pen, with a trimmed path animation and a small ink-dot nib that leads the line and lifts off at each letter's end.
wow: The traveling pen-nib dot and natural stroke-order reveal make it feel hand-signed in real time, not just a fade-in.
mechanism: The script is a stroke-order Path (Shape) drawn with .trim(from:0,to:progress).stroke(); an animated progress drives the reveal while a small nib circle is positioned at the path point for the current trim end. The symbolEffect(.drawOn) seed is SF-Symbol-only, so a hand Path is used instead. iOS 17.
reference: Handwriting Draw-On (symbolEffect drawOn) pattern, done with Path trim
previewLoop: A repeating .animation drives trim progress 0 to 1 then resets on a ~3s loop, so the headline keeps drawing itself on with the nib leading.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Liquid Fill Letters
behavior: Each glyph is masked by a rising liquid level with a wobbling sine surface and a couple of buoyant bubbles, filling from empty outline to full color left-to-right, with a subtle meniscus highlight at the waterline.
wow: The wobbling animated waterline and rising bubbles inside the exact glyph shape make the type look genuinely filled with liquid, all via TextRenderer masking.
mechanism: Outline Text layered under a filled Text masked by a TextRenderer; a TimelineView drives a rising waterline whose surface is a sine-wobbled Path drawn in a Canvas (with small bubble circles), clipped to the glyph shapes. iOS 18 (TextRenderer).
previewLoop: The TimelineView raises the water level 0 to full then resets on a ~3s loop, the sine surface and bubbles animating continuously so the letters keep filling.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: TextRenderer requires iOS 18; tune the sine-surface Path and clip so the waterline reads cleanly inside thin glyph strokes

name: Magnetic Poetry
behavior: Word tiles scatter and tumble in like fridge magnets, each settling with an overshoot wobble, and a drag lets you fling any tile so neighbors physically nudge aside before snapping back to the baseline.
wow: The fling-and-shove neighbor physics make the words feel like real magnets on a metal door, with weight and friction you can feel through your thumb.
mechanism: Word tiles as positioned views with @State offsets; a DragGesture moves the grabbed tile while neighbors recompute a falloff-based shove offset and return on a spring(bounce:); sensoryFeedback fires on settle. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView auto-shuffle: every ~1.5s one tile is programmatically flung to a new slot and springs back, so tiles keep nudging neighbors and resettling without a finger.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture on each tile: translation drives that tile's offset, neighbors apply a distance-falloff shove, and on release every tile springs(bounce:0.4) back to its baseline slot.
risk: cap tile count (~8-12 words) so the neighbor falloff recompute stays cheap

name: 3D Cylinder Marquee
behavior: A scrolling ticker of text wraps around an invisible horizontal cylinder, so words curve away with perspective scale, fade, and blur at the rim as they rotate off one edge and arrive on the other.
wow: The perspective curl and rim blur turn a flat news-ticker into a rotating drum you can flick to spin faster or slower.
mechanism: Each word is positioned by its angle on a virtual cylinder; rotation3DEffect(.y) with .projection perspective plus angle-derived scaleEffect, opacity and .blur give the rim curl, all advanced by a single rotating phase. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView advances the cylinder phase at a constant rate, so words endlessly curl off one edge and arrive on the other inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture x adds to the cylinder phase to scrub/flick the drum; on release angular momentum decays via a TimelineView so it coasts to a stop.

name: Neon Sign Flicker
behavior: A glowing neon-tube headline buzzes to life with a realistic startup flicker, where one stubborn letter stutters on and off a few times before catching, then settles into a steady warm-glow hum with a faint pulsing halo.
wow: The single misbehaving letter that flickers before committing captures the imperfect charm of a real bar-window neon sign.
mechanism: Per-character Text with a stacked .shadow glow (multiple radii) for the tube halo; a KeyframeAnimator scripts an irregular on/off opacity track for the chosen stutter letter, then a low-amplitude TimelineView sine pulses the halo for the steady hum. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator replays the buzz-to-life: dark, the stutter letter flickers, all letters catch, then the halo hums and it loops every ~3s.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture replays the startup-flicker KeyframeAnimator sequence from dark; same script as the preview.

name: Odometer Blur
behavior: A numeric value rolls like a mechanical odometer where each digit wheel spins on a vertical drum, and the fastest-moving wheels gain a vertical motion blur and slight drum curvature shading so they read as physical cylinders.
wow: The per-wheel velocity-driven motion blur and curved-drum shadow sell a spinning mechanical cylinder, not a CSS counter.
mechanism: Per-digit vertical VStack of 0-9 in a clipped window, offset animated by the digit delta; a velocity-proportional .blur(radius:) on the y-axis plus a top/bottom LinearGradient overlay fakes drum curvature. contentTransition(.numericText) is the reference baseline but does not give per-wheel blur, so a custom rolling-drum view is needed. iOS 17.
reference: Stocks / Wallet numeric roll; mechanical car odometer
previewLoop: A TimelineView increments the value on a loop (e.g. +137 every ~1.2s) so the digit drums keep spinning with blur and re-settling on their own.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Ransom Note Assembly
behavior: Letters fly in one by one as mismatched cut-out clippings, each with a random font, rotation, paper torn-edge, and drop shadow, slapping onto the page with a quick scale-overshoot stamp.
wow: The randomized per-letter typeface, tilt, and torn-paper shadow make every replay a unique collage with genuine analog charm.
mechanism: HStack of per-character Text views, each seeded with a random .font, rotationEffect, padding rect background and shadow; each enters via a staggered .transition(.scale) on a spring(bounce:) overshoot. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator re-rolls the random seed and replays the per-letter slap-in stagger every ~2.5s, so a fresh collage assembles on a loop inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture re-seeds the randomness and replays the staggered scale-overshoot slap-in; same sequence as the preview.

name: Glass Shatter Settle
behavior: The headline appears pre-shattered as offset angular shards that fly back together from the edges, each shard rotating and decelerating into perfect alignment, with a brief chromatic edge-light glint as they seat.
wow: Reassembling glass shards with rotational deceleration and an edge glint reads like time-reversed breaking, premium and cinematic.
mechanism: A TextRenderer draws the glyphs clipped into angular shard polygons; each shard starts offset/rotated and is interpolated home by animatableData on a decelerating spring, with a brief gradient edge-glint overlay timed to the seat. iOS 18 (TextRenderer).
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator runs shatter-out then assemble-in on a ~3s loop, shards flying back together and glinting as they seat, no input needed.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture re-fires the shards from their scattered offsets back into alignment with the decelerating spring and edge glint; same animatableData sequence as the preview.

name: Split-Flap Departure
behavior: Each character animates as a mechanical airport split-flap card: the top half hinges down with a 3D rotation while the next glyph flips in, cascading left-to-right with a per-tile delay until the word locks in.
wow: The double-hinge 3D fold and the rapid mid-flip blur of intermediate letters nail the tactile clack of a real Solari board, far past a plain digit roll.
mechanism: Per-character HStack of two stacked half-glyph clips (top/bottom split via .mask), driven by a KeyframeAnimator angle track applying rotation3DEffect(.x) with .projection perspective; a per-tile delay staggers the cascade and an interim random glyph swaps at the 90 degree midpoint. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator cycles a target word every ~2s, auto-firing the flap cascade left-to-right so the tile board keeps flipping to new short words inside the 116-160pt tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture re-fires the full flap cascade to a new word; same KeyframeAnimator-per-tile sequence as the preview, just triggered by the tap instead of the timer.

name: Spotlight Reveal
behavior: A soft radial spotlight glides across a dim embossed headline, brightening and lifting only the characters it currently illuminates with a slight scale and shadow, leaving a warm afterglow trail behind it.
wow: Dragging your own spotlight across the words, with letters rising into the light and fading as it passes, feels like reading by candlelight.
mechanism: A dim base Text under a bright copy masked by a moving RadialGradient; per-character distance to the spotlight x drives a small scaleEffect and shadow lift, with a trailing lower-opacity afterglow gradient. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView sweeps the spotlight x from left to right and back on a ~3s loop, letters rising and dimming as it passes inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: DragGesture x sets the spotlight center; characters near it brighten, scale and lift while a warm afterglow trails the touch point.

name: Rubber Stamp Slam
behavior: The word slams down as an over-rotated ink stamp that overshoots in scale, kicks up a faint dust ring, and leaves a textured uneven-ink imprint with slightly heavier edges and a tiny settle wobble.
wow: The scale overshoot, dust puff, and patchy ink coverage make it feel like a heavy rubber stamp physically struck the screen.
mechanism: A KeyframeAnimator slams scale from large to 1 with a slight rotationEffect overshoot and settle wobble; the dust ring is an expanding/fading stroked Circle, and patchy ink is a static noise/grain overlay masked by the Text. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A PhaseAnimator replays the slam (scale overshoot + dust ring + wobble) every ~2.5s so the stamp keeps striking the page in the tile.
interactiveSpec: onTapGesture replays the KeyframeAnimator slam with the dust-ring puff and settle wobble; same sequence as the preview.

name: Typewriter Carriage
behavior: Characters type in one at a time with a blinking block cursor and a subtle per-key vertical jitter, and at line end the whole line shifts with a carriage-return slide and a soft mechanical settle before the next line types.
wow: The carriage-return line shift and tiny per-keystroke jitter add the mechanical imperfection that lifts it above a plain reveal.
mechanism: A Timer/TimelineView advances a visible-character count over a prefix of the string; each new glyph gets a tiny y jitter via a brief offset animation, a block-cursor Rectangle blinks via a repeating opacity, and line completion triggers a carriage-return offset slide with a spring settle. iOS 17.
previewLoop: The TimelineView types the line, blinks the cursor, does the carriage-return slide, then clears and retypes on a loop inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Variable Weight Breathe
behavior: The headline continuously inhales and exhales by interpolating a variable font's weight axis under TimelineView, with a soft phase offset per word so the line breathes like a living organism.
wow: True continuous weight interpolation (not discrete .bold steps) makes the type physically swell and slim, a buttery effect almost nobody implements correctly.
mechanism: TimelineView drives a sine phase; each word's Font is rebuilt every frame from a UIFont with a variation dictionary on the wght axis (Font(UIFont)) since SwiftUI weight is not natively Animatable, with a per-word phase offset. iOS 17.
previewLoop: The TimelineView sine runs forever, so the per-word weight axis swells and slims on a continuous breathing loop inside the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: variable-font weight axis is not natively Animatable; needs a UIFont variation-dictionary bridge rebuilt per frame under TimelineView, and a font that actually ships a continuous wght axis

name: Bouncing Payload
behavior: A ball drops under gravity, squashes on impact with the floor and stretches at the apex, losing a little height each bounce until it auto-resets and drops again.
wow: Classic squash-and-stretch with damped bounce height gives it cartoon weight, and you can fling it sideways to add horizontal travel before it settles back to a clean loop.
mechanism: KeyframeAnimator with independent tracks: vertical position (gravity arc), and scaleX/scaleY for squash-on-impact / stretch-at-apex, with decreasing peak height per bounce. A blurred Ellipse shadow scales with height. iOS 17.
reference: Keyframe Bounce Loader; classic squash-and-stretch ball
previewLoop: KeyframeAnimator loops the drop→squash→bounce→reset cycle automatically so the ball keeps hopping with damped height.
interactiveSpec: Detail adds a DragGesture: a sideways flick at release gives horizontal velocity (friction-decayed via TimelineView) layered onto the vertical bounce loop, so the ball travels then settles back to the clean loop.

name: Coin Flip Stack
behavior: A coin repeatedly flips on its horizontal axis using rotation3DEffect with perspective, casting a foreshortened shadow that stretches and shrinks as the coin turns edge-on.
wow: Flinging spins it multiple revolutions with momentum decay before settling face-up, and the synced shadow distortion makes the flat disc feel like solid metal catching light.
mechanism: rotation3DEffect(.degrees(angle), axis:(1,0,0)) with a perspective anchorZ; the face swaps (heads/tails) at the 90° crossing via opacity. The shadow is a blurred Ellipse whose width scales with cos(angle). TimelineView spins it idle; a DragGesture's release velocity feeds a friction-decayed angle integrator. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView spins the coin's X-axis angle continuously so it flips and the shadow pulses on its own.
interactiveSpec: Detail uses a DragGesture: vertical flick velocity at release seeds a spin that decays under friction (TimelineView integrator) over several revolutions before easing to a face-up rest.

name: Comet Trail
behavior: A bright comet head orbits an elliptical path leaving a tapering particle tail that always streams away from the focal center, the head accelerating at perihelion like a real orbit.
wow: The tail's length and brightness pulse with orbital speed and it points away from the center the way a solar wind tail would, giving a tiny piece of astrophysics charm.
mechanism: Parametric ellipse for the head position with a non-uniform time mapping (faster near perihelion, à la Kepler). The tail is a Canvas trail of fading particles spawned each frame, each pushed radially away from the focal center; head brightness/tail length scale with speed. TimelineView drives it. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView advances the orbital parameter forever so the comet laps the ellipse with its speed-modulated tail — self-driving.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: DNA Helix
behavior: Two phosphate strands corkscrew as a double helix with base-pair rungs connecting them, scaling each rung by a sine of its phase so the rotation reads as genuine 3D twist.
wow: Dragging horizontally scrubs the twist speed and direction like spinning a physical model, and the depth-based rung scaling plus opacity makes the helix convincingly volumetric.
mechanism: Vertical ForEach of rungs; each strand node's x = sin(y*freq + phase) and the rung width/opacity scale by that sine to fake depth, with nearer rungs drawn on top. TimelineView advances phase when idle; a DragGesture's accumulated translation drives phase when touched. iOS 17.
reference: science/biotech intro animations; lab app loaders
previewLoop: TimelineView advances the helix phase at a steady rate so the strands corkscrew continuously, depth scaling pulsing the rungs — self-driving.
interactiveSpec: Detail wires a DragGesture: horizontal translation adds to the twist phase (velocity sets scrub speed and sign); releasing hands control back to the idle TimelineView spin.

name: Dot Grid Ripple
behavior: A 2D grid of dots pulses in size and brightness as a circular wavefront sweeps outward from a moving origin, each dot delayed by its distance from the wave center.
wow: Tapping spawns a new wave origin at your finger so it feels like dropping a stone on a pegboard, and untouched it auto-emits waves from drifting origins.
mechanism: Canvas draws the dot grid; for each dot, scale/opacity = a sine of (elapsedTime - distance(dot, origin)*k), so the phase ripples outward. TimelineView(.animation) supplies elapsed time; tap origins are stored with their start time. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView emits waves from a slowly drifting origin point so ripples sweep the grid continuously without any touch.
interactiveSpec: Detail adds a SpatialTapGesture: each tap records a new origin + timestamp at the touch location, spawning a fresh expanding wavefront layered over the idle drift.

name: Flip-Clock Counter
behavior: A split-flap display counts a percentage upward, each digit's top half hinging down over the bottom with a 3D fold and a tiny bounce as the new number snaps into place.
wow: The mechanical split-flap fold with the hinge shadow nails that retro airport-board feel, making determinate progress feel physical instead of a boring number.
mechanism: Each digit is two stacked half-tiles (top/bottom clip of the glyph); the falling top half animates rotation3DEffect(axis:(1,0,0)) from 0°→-90° with a perspective anchor and a hinge-shadow gradient, snapping with a spring bounce. A value change re-triggers the fold per changed digit. iOS 17.
previewLoop: A TimelineView or repeating timer increments the percentage 0→100→0 so the digits keep flapping over on their own.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: the hinge fold needs correct half-tile clipping + perspective anchorZ so the top flap reads as folding toward the viewer, not just shrinking

name: Origami Fold
behavior: A flat square creases and folds along diagonals through a sequence of origami steps into a crane-like form, then unfolds back to flat, looping the fold-unfold cycle.
wow: Triangular facets shading lighter and darker as they tilt sell the paper folding in 3D, an unexpectedly elegant loader that looks like real origami in motion.
mechanism: Triangular facets are Paths; each fold animates a facet's rotation3DEffect about its crease edge (axis + anchor) through scripted keyframes via KeyframeAnimator, with per-facet fill brightness driven by the fold angle to fake directional lighting. iOS 17.
reference: Pinch Origami Fold; paper-fold logo reveals
previewLoop: KeyframeAnimator auto-plays the fold→hold→unfold sequence on a loop so the square keeps folding into the crane and back.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: fold math is fiddly — each facet's rotation anchor must sit on its shared crease edge or facets detach; keep the step count modest

name: Gear Train
behavior: Three meshed gears of different tooth counts rotate together, each turning at the correct inverse-ratio speed and direction so the teeth stay perfectly interlocked as they spin.
wow: The mathematically correct gear ratios mean the teeth never clip or slip, and a rotate gesture lets you crank the whole train by hand like a real mechanism.
mechanism: Each gear is an Animatable Shape (teeth via a path of radial trapezoids); driving angle θ sets gear2 = -θ·(t1/t2) and gear3 = θ·(t1/t3) so meshing stays exact. TimelineView drives θ when idle; a RotationGesture (or DragGesture atan2 about the hub) drives θ when cranked. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView advances the drive gear's angle steadily so all three meshed gears spin at their correct ratios continuously.
interactiveSpec: Detail wires a RotationGesture (or DragGesture using atan2 around the drive hub): the user's rotation sets the drive angle θ and the dependent gears follow by ratio; releasing resumes the idle spin.
risk: tooth phase must be offset per gear so teeth interlock — derive each gear's starting rotation from its tooth count, not zero

name: Infinity Flow
behavior: A glowing bead races along a lemniscate (figure-eight) path while a gradient stroke trails behind it, the bead speeding up at the crossover pinch and easing on the loops.
wow: Variable speed mapped to path curvature makes the bead feel like it has momentum on a real track, and the self-crossing trail layers over itself for a hypnotic ribbon.
mechanism: A lemniscate Path drawn with an Angular/LinearGradient stroke; the bead position uses the parametric figure-eight equations (x=cos t, y=sin t·cos t) with a non-linear t mapping to speed up at the crossover. TimelineView advances t; the trail is a trim'd sub-path behind the bead. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView advances the path parameter t forever so the bead laps the figure-eight with its trailing ribbon — self-driving.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Braiding Threads
behavior: Three colored strands weave over and under each other in a continuous braid that scrolls upward, with correct alternating z-order at every crossing so the plait reads as woven.
wow: Per-crossing over/under z-ordering produces a genuinely woven braid rather than overlapping lines, an unusually tactile, textile-like loader.
mechanism: Each strand is a sine Path offset in phase by 120°; the braid scrolls by advancing a vertical phase via TimelineView. Over/under is achieved by drawing each strand in short segments and ordering segment draw calls per crossing (segment whose sin is frontmost drawn last), inside a single Canvas. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView scrolls the braid's vertical phase upward continuously so the three strands weave forever — self-driving.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: per-crossing z-order correctness is fiddly — segment the strands finely enough that the over/under swaps land exactly at the crossing points

name: Liquid Flask Fill
behavior: A rounded container fills from the bottom with tinted liquid whose surface tilts and sloshes with inertia, settling level after a spring overshoot, with tiny bubbles rising as it fills.
wow: The surface reads the device's roll via Core Motion so the liquid genuinely tips toward gravity, and in a grid tile it auto-sloshes on a timer so it still feels alive without sensors.
mechanism: An Animatable Shape draws the liquid as a sine-wave surface whose phase and tilt angle are animatable; fill level animates with a bouncy spring overshoot. A Canvas or ForEach of small circles rises as bubbles via TimelineView. The flask outline is a clipped RoundedRectangle/Capsule. iOS 17.
previewLoop: TimelineView nudges the surface tilt with a slow sine and steps the fill level up on a timer so the liquid sloshes and rises on its own — no sensors in the tile.
interactiveSpec: Detail uses a DragGesture: horizontal translation maps to surface tilt angle and vertical drag adjusts fill level; on release a spring settles the surface back to level with overshoot. (Core Motion roll is an optional real-device enhancement, not shipped in the paste-ready view.)
risk: Core Motion is dead in preview/sim, so ship the DragGesture mapping; gate CMMotionManager behind availability and a fallback

name: Magnetic Filings
behavior: Scattered iron-filing slivers align their angles to follow curved field lines around a hidden magnet pole that orbits the center, the slivers rotating in waves as the field moves.
wow: Dragging moves the magnet pole and the whole field of slivers swings to point at your finger, an eerily physical magnetism effect that auto-orbits when idle.
mechanism: Canvas draws ~hundreds of short line slivers on a grid; each sliver's angle = atan2 toward the pole position (dipole-ish field). TimelineView orbits the pole when idle; a DragGesture sets the pole to the finger location, and slivers re-align with an eased lerp toward target angle. iOS 17.
reference: iron filings + magnet science demo; field-line visualizers
previewLoop: TimelineView orbits the hidden pole around the center so all slivers swing to follow the moving field — self-driving with no touch.
interactiveSpec: Detail wires a DragGesture: the pole snaps to the finger position and every sliver re-points toward it (eased), so dragging visibly drags the whole field; release resumes the idle orbit.
risk: perf: cap sliver count and batch in one Canvas; recompute angles per frame is O(n), keep grid coarse enough to stay 60fps on device

name: Metaball Orbit
behavior: Two gooey blobs chase each other around a ring; when they meet they fuse into a single liquid mass with a bridging neck, then split apart with surface tension as the loop repeats.
wow: The merge-and-split goo is pure Canvas using blur plus an alphaThreshold filter, so the metaball physics looks expensive but ships with zero Metal.
mechanism: Canvas wraps the two filled circles in addFilter(.blur(radius:)) then addFilter(.alphaThreshold(min:)) inside a drawLayer so overlapping blurred edges snap into one solid metaball with a connecting neck. TimelineView animates each blob's angle around a shared ring. iOS 17 (Canvas filters available 15+).
reference: Dynamic Island gooey merge; iMessage blob morph
previewLoop: TimelineView orbits both blobs around the ring at offset phases so they periodically fuse and split on their own — fully self-driving in the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: metaball needs Canvas blur + alphaThreshold tuned together; verify the threshold/blur ratio on device since simulator anti-aliasing differs

name: Morse Pulse
behavior: A single bar emits a looping rhythm of long dashes and short dots like a Morse transmission, the bar glowing and stretching for each pulse with crisp gaps between symbols.
wow: Encoding an actual word in Morse turns waiting into a hidden message, a witty, minimalist loader with personality and a deliberately uneven, human rhythm.
mechanism: An array of dot/dash/gap durations defines the timeline; TimelineView(.animation) reads elapsed time, looks up the current symbol's on/off state, and drives the bar's width/opacity/glow (shadow). A Capsule with animated frame width is enough. iOS 17.
reference: Morse code / telegraph motif; minimalist signal loaders
previewLoop: TimelineView cycles the Morse symbol schedule on a loop so the bar blinks the encoded word forever — self-driving.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Orbital Electrons
behavior: Three electron dots travel along tilted elliptical orbits around a glowing nucleus, each orbit on its own period so they weave in front of and behind the core.
wow: True z-ordering as dots pass behind the nucleus sells real 3D depth from flat 2D ellipses, and the slight orbital eccentricity gives it an analog, hand-tuned feel.
mechanism: TimelineView(.animation) advances a phase; each dot's x/y comes from parametric ellipse equations (cos/sin) scaled by per-orbit tilt, with a RadialGradient nucleus. Z-order is faked by splitting each orbit's stroke into a behind-pass and in-front-pass drawn before/after the nucleus based on the sin of the phase. iOS 17.
reference: Rutherford/atom logo motifs; Apple's loading orbit aesthetics
previewLoop: TimelineView drives the orbital phase continuously at a fixed period so all three dots weave around the nucleus forever — already self-driving, no gesture needed.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop

name: Particle Assemble
behavior: A scattered cloud of dozens of particles flies inward and snaps into a recognizable glyph or ring with a spring settle, holds briefly, then explodes back into the cloud to loop.
wow: The assemble-hold-disperse cycle feels like a thing being born and dissolving, and each particle's stagger plus spring overshoot makes the formation land with real weight.
mechanism: Each particle has a precomputed scatter point and a target point sampled along a glyph/ring path; a PhaseAnimator cycles scatter→assembled→hold→scatter and interpolates each particle's position with per-index stagger and a spring. Particles render in a single Canvas for cheap batch drawing. iOS 17.
reference: Particle Assemble Check (Apple-Pay-style success); logo-reveal intros
previewLoop: PhaseAnimator auto-runs the scatter→assemble→hold→disperse phases on a loop so the glyph forms and dissolves repeatedly in the tile.
interactiveSpec: auto — same as previewLoop
risk: perf: cap particle count (~60-80) and draw all in one Canvas; avoid one View per particle

name: Newton's Cradle
behavior: Five suspended balls swing as a Newton's cradle: the end ball arcs in, momentum transfers through the stationary middle, and the far ball kicks out, alternating sides forever.
wow: Dragging lifts and releases an end ball with real pendulum easing, and the dead-still middle balls with only the ends moving nails the physics everyone recognizes.
mechanism: Five balls on string lines; only the two end balls' rotation angles animate, driven by a KeyframeAnimator (or PhaseAnimator) with eased keyframes for the lift→strike→kick cycle while the middle three stay fixed. Strings are Path lines rotated about the top anchor. iOS 17.
reference: Newton's cradle desk toy; physics-demo loaders
